product_name: Apple EarPods Headphones with USB-C Plug, Wired Ear Buds with Built-in Remote to Control Music, Phone Calls, and Volume
title: The $9 Audiophile Secret: Small Dongle, Big Practicality
body: Lets be honest: nobody actually wants to live the "dongle life." When the headphone jack started disappearing from our devices, most of us were annoyed at the thought of yet another accessory to lose. But after spending a significant amount of time with the Apple USB-C to 3.5 mm Headphone Jack Adapter, Ive realized its less of an annoyance and more of a practical hero in my daily toolkit. Whether Im grading student dissertations or jumping between devices for lectures, this little white cable has become surprisingly indispensable. The "Hidden" Audio Quality As someone who appreciates high-fidelity gear, I was skeptical. Ive used high-end headphones from brands like Beyerdynamic and Audio-Technica, and usually, youd expect a $9 adapter to muddy the sound. Consumer feedback often points this out, but the reality is a pleasant surprise: this isn't just a physical adapter; its a tiny, highly efficient Digital-to-Analog Converter (DAC). In the audiophile community, this dongle is actually legendary for its transparency. It has an incredibly low output impedance, which means if youre like me and enjoy using sensitive in-ear monitors (IEMs), you wont hear that annoying background hiss that plagues cheaper adapters. The sound is neutral and cleanit doesn't try to "color" the music, which is exactly what I want when Im focusing on work. Read more Read more

product_name: Apple EarPods Headphones with USB-C Plug, Wired Ear Buds with Built-in Remote to Control Music, Phone Calls, and Volume
title: Fit issues
body: If youve used an iPhone from before the USB-C switch, you already know these EarPods. Theyre plug-and-play and easy to use. The sound is decent, with solid bass and clear treble for a basic wired pair. The main issues are fit, durability, and price. The fit isnt great for everyone. The angle doesnt always sit right, so they can sound a bit thin unless you adjust them. I usually need earbud covers to keep them in place, and even then I sometimes have to press them slightly forward or back to get the best sound. Durability is hit or miss. After about a year, the rubber near the Y-junction where the wires split tends to loosen. Once that happens, the wiring feels exposed and more fragile. Ive had a few pairs fail this way, and its usually cheaper to replace them than repair them. The price also feels high for such an old design. Theyre okay and mostly reliable, but the fit issues, weak point at the Y-junction, and unchanged pricing are real drawbacks.

title: My Thoughts As a Samsung "Fanboy"
body: Like the title says, I'm a Samsung guy through and through. But, out of curiosity, I ordered these EarPods just to try them out. I gotta say, these are really good earbuds, especially for the price. They fit perfectly in my ears, and works with most USB C devices. I use them with my Galaxy S24 Ultra, and my older Tab S7 without any issues. My brother recently got an iPhone, and said they work perfectly well (of course) for him. Sound quality is pretty good regardless of what you listen to. While they're not made for audiophiles, I found these to be above average relative to other earbuds in the same price range. The built-in mic works fine for phone calls and video chatting, and never had anyone complain about any sort of issue on their end. I like that it has buttons for volume, media control, etc. Noise cancellation isn't the best, but it's to expected due to how they fit in your ears. All in all, whether you're on Team Apple or Team Android, I highly recommend these EarPods!

title: Nice upgrade to a very useful device
body: I love Apple devices and smart home appliances. As such, I was a very early adopter of the original AirTags and have used them while traveling (tucked inside of luggage) and in my daily life for years. The second generation of the AirTag represents a small, but worthwhile change to the function of the device. The ability to precisely track an object is noticeably better and the speaker inside pings very so slightly louder (I personally dont buy the 50% louder claim, but there is a difference). If youre the type to have keys slip behind couch cushions or get buried under laundry, this is the change youve been waiting for. The 2nd generation AirTag keeps the exact same footprint (both weight and size) so it fits perfectly into any existing accessories or holders you might have. Set up is a breeze and the Find My app works like a charm. I was able to see my checked bag move throughout a major airport and its arrival at my destination using the app. You can also disable forgotten item notifications easily if you happen to be leaving luggage in a hotel and dont want to be pinged if you head out for the day. This AirTag weighs next to nothing and is worth slipping into a bag or attaching to a keychain for peace of mind. It seems just as durable as the first generation which I have toted all over the planet. Read more Read more

title: Simple, Reliable Peace of Mind
body: Setup was effortless. My phone detected the tag automatically and walked me through the whole process. If that doesn't happen for some reason, you can add it manually through the Find My app, but either way it takes about a minute. Just keep in mind you need an iPhone for any of this to work. I use mine on my dog's collar as a safety net in case he ever gets out and gets lost. Thankfully I haven't had to use it in an emergency yet, but I've tested it and it works exactly as advertised. There's real peace of mind in knowing it's there. One thing worth mentioning that doesn't get talked about enough: the battery needs to be changed periodically. It's a simple swap and the tag will notify you when it's getting low, but if you ignore those alerts and the battery dies, the tag is useless when you need it most. Easy to manage, just don't set it and forget it completely. Overall a great product. Simple to use and does exactly what it promises.

product_name: Apple AirPods 4 Wireless Earbuds, Bluetooth Headphones, Personalized Spatial Audio, Sweat and Water Resistant, USB-C Charging Case, H2 Chip, Up to 30 Hours of Battery Life, Effortless Setup for iPhone
title: All day comfort and reliable sound that feels effortless
body: I bought these because I wanted a pair of wireless earbuds that could handle long listening sessions, workouts, and everyday calls without constant fiddling. The redesign with a shorter stem and a softer contour looked like it would sit more stably in my ears, and the quick press controls seemed handy for skipping tracks or taking a call on the go. Pairing in day to day use was straightforward. I just opened the charging case near my phone and tapped connect, and they were ready to go. They stay in place during workouts and commutes, and the ear sensor pauses audio when I take them out which is convenient for quick interruptions or when I need to listen to something short without fumbling with the device. What stood out most is the overall clarity of calls and the way music and shows feel more immersive with spatial audio that moves with my head. The sound isn't harsh at higher volumes and the voice isolation helps keep conversations clear even in busy environments. Battery life is solid too with about five hours per charge and a total of around 30 hours with the case, and the charging case itself charges via USB-C which is convenient during travel. A small caveat is the learning curve with the quick press controls, which took a moment to get used to. The case is noticeably smaller than the previous generation, but it still adds a bit of bulk when tucked into a pocket. Overall, they feel reliable, comfortable, and genuinely enjoyable to use, delivering a five star experience. Read more Read more

product_name: Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds, Active Noise Cancellation, Live Translation, Heart Rate Sensing, Hearing Aid Feature, Bluetooth Headphones, Spatial Audio, High-Fidelity Sound, USB-C Charging
title: Best AirPods Ever
body: Best decision ever to buy these. Your voice is isolated and nobody can hear ANYTHING going on in the background. So if you're on an important call and the dog barks, no big deal. If you are walking outside, wind, music, other voices - all filtered out. If you're starving and can't help but rudely eat a cracker on call - they can't hear you crunch! The other reason these airpods are awesome is because they FIT! The original AirPods pro did not fit me at all. I bought foam tips. Fail. I bought special over the ear hooks. Fail. So I just muddled my way through until they finally didn't work anymore and then I switched to Beats for a a few years. What changed it all? Talking with my bestie on the phone was driving me crazy. She was always on the move and making tons of noise that was ear piercing for me. When she unloaded the dishwasher it was a special kind of hell in my ears. She shopped for ear buds that would save my sanity, and she hit the jackpot with these. We spoke the other day and I was in shock - I couldn't hear her dog barking, her dishes clanking or....the final convincing moment: I could not hear her blowdryer! (I asked her to turn it on as a test). I had to buy them! I read reviews that said the fit was better so I figured I would give them a try. Last thing: This version has features I never knew I needed but LOVE. I was listening to an audiobook when I saw someone and started chatting and the book automatically stopped playing until the conversation was over. The Pro 3 also includes a hearing test and measures to protect your precious ears. Do I work for Apple? NO! I just wanted to share my enthusiasm to take the guesswork out of this purchase for you. They are pricey (if you didn't agree you wouldn't read a review this long), but they are worth it! They work great (seamless with an iPhone) and fit great. Now I just have to take really good care of them and make sure they last and don't get lost. Read more Read more

product_name: Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds, Active Noise Cancellation, Live Translation, Heart Rate Sensing, Hearing Aid Feature, Bluetooth Headphones, Spatial Audio, High-Fidelity Sound, USB-C Charging
title: A Solid Product At An Incredible Price
body: I've had two sets of professional hearing aids in the past, one of them being fairly new, but these are my new favorite even though they are far less expensive. I will warn that pairing to Android devices can be somewhat difficult (seems to work best if you pair before pairing to your Apple device), but I was able to pair to both my Samsung S25+ and Samsung Watch 8 Classic (the 25+ being the more difficult). Particularly nice is "Adaptive" mode, which changes the setting automatically when it hears real conversation (not just TV conversation), but oddly you have to change the setup so that you can switch to that mode by just touching the device. That is a real game changer, because it eliminates the stray white-type noise you hear with hearing aids, and you just hear your surroundings in a passthrough mode. Battery life seems to exceed the 10 hours claimed using them in Adaptive mode with the hearing aid function on. The hearing aid testing was fairly easy, but I question its accuracy since it showed too similar of results for my left and right ear, when there is a difference at high frequencies. Doesn't seem to affect the overall operation though. My main complaint which almost results in a one star reduction is the lousy user documentation. Rather than just having a webpage that covers this item it's combined with all the other similar devices. And it really doesn't explain that well what the various settings do. Finally, it doesn't seem to be available in just a PDF version that you can download. Another item to note is switching between Android devices is manual, although that may be an advantage. product_name: Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds, Active Noise Cancellation, Live Translation, Heart Rate Sensing, Hearing Aid Feature, Bluetooth Headphones, Spatial Audio, High-Fidelity Sound, USB-C Charging
title: A subtle but significant upgrade for iOS users.**UPDATE**
body: I am an ear bud junky and have been on a quest for the perfect set for a long time. I have owned and own a lot of the big names from Apple, B&0, B&W, Sony, Bose, Klipsch, Devialet, Technics, many off brands, and more. I use both iOS and Android devices and dont have a particular favorite so I can speak to how ear buds interact with both. This is an interesting update. I almost passed on it and I probably should have as my AirPod Pro 2s are just fine. However, they dont fit me perfectly and there has been a lot made of the new fit these have. My oldest son will be getting the 2s. These look and feel a lot like the 2s. The physical design is nearly identical. However, the upgrades, while subtle, are mostly notable. I will break these down below and try to help those that are deciding on whether the upgrade is worth your money. Sound: Those that have read any of my other reviews on buds, know I always start with sound. It is the primary function of an ear bud. Apple has a new retuned driver here and it is a good one. The sound is natural and has good definition. You will feel the bass and highs are crispy. It is hard to define how much better these are from the 2s but they seem louder and have more clarity. A lot of this probably has to do with the new seal these give you in your ears as well (more on that later) but these are well tuned and are noticeably improved. This was an area that needed to improve as most other high end buds were better. Sony, Technics, and Bose were all better sounding at similar prices. Some of them offer higher end codecs for more lossless streams which Apple appears to still be avoiding. I still think there are better sounding buds out there but Apple has closed the gap in meaningful ways. Well done. Read more Read more

product_name: Apple AirPods Pro 3 Wireless Earbuds, Active Noise Cancellation, Live Translation, Heart Rate Sensing, Hearing Aid Feature, Bluetooth Headphones, Spatial Audio, High-Fidelity Sound, USB-C Charging
title: 5 stars, but hedge your expectations against Apple audio products
body: Best AirPods Ive ever owned. I needed these for Spaces on X (Twitter), and the audio quality is the most pristine of all AirPods Ive had. I am a bit of an audiophile, so they are not necessarily the highest tier audio quality you could purchase for this price. If you want something better for the price without caring if whether or not they are AirPods, youre better-off looking at anything from Sennheiser. Thus, the value for the money is absolutely as inflated as you could imagine for a typical Apple product. Ive noticed that these fit and stay in my ears much better than previous AirPods Ive owned before. What actually makes these special is the ability to set up realistic spatial sound. If you take the time to set it up when you get these, you will be very happy with how insanely immersive these are. You turn your head in any direction, and you will hear the audio as if you are near it, and you will hear through one ear a little more than the other as you move your head in different directions. Its a genius implementation for the purpose of immersive audio, and I love it very much. This can be shut off in Settings if you dont want this feature. I should mention that this only occurs for the Music app (Im not sure about Spotify or other streaming platforms, so DYOR), and not for other audio sources like YouTube or X Spaces. If you can afford this premium price, I would say go ahead and get them. However, it really needs to be said again that Apple product prices are inherently inflated. I have no intention of testing durability at all whatsoever because I ordered a shell for the AirPods case, but I will say that you definitely do not want to drop AirPods on hard surfaces. I dropped one AirPod several years ago when I owned the first generation, and that AirPod broke. Treat them with delicacy, because they generally are not very durable. Stay safe by purchasing AppleCare coverage for these AirPods. If you are already willing to spend this much on earbuds, then you have no excuse to not buy the coverage. Read more Read more

title: Great earbuds.
body: I bought these to replace my Nothing Ear headphones, they were ruined by a run or two through the wash. The Airpods Pro 3 work really well, the active noise cancelling does a good job of blocking out regular sounds like vibrations, some conversations, etc. They do seem to struggle to block the sound of people coughing. I was able to test them in a loud environment the other day when I was operating a garden tractor to plow my drive, they weren't able to cancel out the very loud tractor, but they did reduce the sound significantly. I was able to enjoy my music while plowing with the volume only at 50% or so. I did also have these headphones washed by my wife, and they made it through one wash cycle with no noticeable change to the performance. I did pay for Apple care for these since I know I'm prone to leaving things in my pockets and I know my wife doesn't check pockets. Hopefully I'll never have to use that, but we'll see. title: Awesome reader
body: Love it 90% of the time. Reading on it is pretty great and it's very sturdy. Ive read around 500 books on it so far and if you just want it to read it's hard to compare the battery life and general ease of use. I don't have a screen protector and I don't have the case and I can confidently say this thing doesn't need that protection. Ive dropped it, chipped the outside edge bumper somehow and it's been smashed at least a few times. Works fine, Its pretty flexible and I think that saves it from most damage that would otherwise mess with it. It does have some issues though, Navigating the menus could be better. The touch controls can be a little frustrating sometimes even when I know how they are supposed to work. Like accidentally clicking links in books when Im trying to turn to a new page or go back to my library. Book covers look meh at best on it and sometimes I look at them using my iPad. My biggest gripe would be one handed reading. If I hold the kindle with my left hand its challenging to turn the page with a finger (the left side of the screen turns back the page) You might be wandering when you would read with one hand, lazy reading on the couch or in bed. It does seem to have issues updating with other readers, like switching between the kindle and my phone or iPad. If I only switched between my phone and tablet my pages would always be about were I left off... not so much with the kindle in the mix. That isn't normally a concern because I switch less now that I have a kindle.. but if you still want to switch devices it isn't updating activity between devices often though that gives an opportunity to read different things on other devices which ive done and that is nice sometimes too. product_name: Amazon Kindle Paperwhite 16GB (newest model) – 20% faster, with new 7" glare-free display and weeks of battery life – Black
title: Hold A Thousand Books in Your Hand
body: Imagine holding a thousand books in your hand. Imagine wearing your jammies while getting that next book in your series, and starting into it a minute later. Imagine reading at night in bed without needing a lamp because your book is backlit (and the light doesnt hurt your eyes). Imagine having your kindle read to you (even if you dont have audiobooks) with text to speech while you drive or fold laundry. The 12th edition of the Kindle Paperwhite is a fabulous device and well-worth considering if you read a lot. Its not too big, not heavy, waterproof, and easy to carry in your purse or backpack. Note that if you get a cover with it (highly recommended), when you open the cover the Kindle turns on automatically to the last page you were reading. With the cover open it feels like youre holding a thin book -- or fold back the cover if you prefer. The Kindle E-reader is not a computer. Its not a tablet or a phone. It is a gizmo that acts like a book with a black-and-white flat e-ink screen showing typed sentences and paragraphs. You turn pages one at a time, just like a book. There is no movement on the page and no distraction or advertisement*. It is just text, plus any images that the author has included to illustrate his book. *NOTE: The advertisements on the Kindle are on the main page not the individual book pages and just show books you might like. They are unobtrusive. Read more Read more

title: Fast easy set up, smooth streaming
body: This is my 6th Amazon Fire TV stick. We have them on 3 TV's in our home and they get some pretty heavy use, so after a couple of years, I have to buy new ones because the old ones either stop working or slow down tremendously. This is a newer version and has HD quality, Alexa voice , pre- installed apps and takes just minutes to set up. This one I purchased to replace the one on my daughters TV in her room and I was about to set it up for her (she's 12) when she says, "I got this mom". "Ok great just let me know if you need help getting it set up and paired because sometimes the volume doesn't Always pair properly on first try". I go about my house work expecting to hear my daughters voice yell "Mom, can you help me"? Never came. My 12 year old set it up all on her own, no help from mom. That is how easy it is to set up the fire TV Stick. Albeit my daughter is a bright gifted Honor Roll student, lol. Seriously though you don't need to be tech savvy to set this up or use it. It's got Alexa voice built in so you can just press the blue button on the remote and ask Alexa to find a program for you. I've noticed zero buffering, connects to our wifi fast and easy and loads apps quickly. Even with our smart TVs, having a fire TV Stick is a plus. But if you have an older TV or an older smart TV that doesn't update anymore, this is the way to go. The design is easy to understand and flows smoothly. Definitely worth every penny. While I was ordering this one I read that Amazon will even take your old fire sticks that don't work anymore. Since I've got a few of those lying around, I think I'll check into it. title: Would recommend as a reliable, cost friendly option in comparison to competitors!
body: I went back and forth on getting this watch for quite a while due to the shorter battery life but Im so glad I took the leap! I had a Garmin that I loved and would have stuck with instead but it doesnt work with Natural Cycles. I went back and forth for well over a month between this and the Oura Ring. Im really pleased with my choice given all the Oura battery failures. Overall, an Apple Watch is an Apple Watch. They are consistent, intuitive, and handy. I shut off all the extra fuss notifications to keep it quieter and its been great as someone who doesnt love constantly being plugged in. The 11 has a better battery than my old 8 I had before I went to Garmin which has been great and it charges fast. Is it as convenient as the DAYS and extreme durability you get from Garmin, definitely not - but it does what its supposed to. Its a clean, minimal looking watch and I appreciate the increased durability. The value for money is almost unmatched- and IS unmatched (in my humble opinion) when you look at the costs of Garmin and Oura these days VS reliability (my husband has the Garmin Instinct and it had nonstop software issues until recently and to fix it we had to go through a whole process and delete files - plug it into a computer etc, Ouras batteries dying within a year). product_name: Apple Watch Series 11 [GPS 42mm] Smartwatch with Rose Gold Aluminum Case with Light Blush Sport Band - S/M. Sleep Score, Fitness Tracker, Health Monitoring, Always-On Display, Water Resistant
title: Solid daily smartwatch that fits real life
body: I bought this smartwatch to get a clearer picture of my sleep, heart activity, and workouts without pulling out my phone all day. The sleep score and health monitoring sounded helpful, and the option to take ECG readings whenever I want and track blood oxygen felt reassuring for daily use. Day to day it works smoothly. The always on display is easy to glance at, and the watch stays comfortable for all day wear and even sleep. It handles workouts with solid metrics, and the band stays secure during runs. Battery life is up to 24 hours of normal use, and a quick 15 minute charge can add several hours. What stood out is how the insights feel actionable rather than just numbers. The alerts for high or low heart rate show up when I need them, and the sleep score lines up with how rested I feel. The durable glass plus a 50 meter water resistance makes it feel ready for everyday life, and safety features like fall detection and automatic check-in when I reach a destination give me extra peace of mind. One small caveat is that the size is a bit bulky on smaller wrists and the wealth of features can take a little time to navigate without checking the app often. It also depends on having it paired with a phone for some functions. Still, in everyday use it feels accurate and reliable, and the overall experience is genuinely satisfying. Overall, it's earned a five-star impression. Read more Read more

product_name: Amazon Echo Dot (newest model) - Vibrant sounding speaker, Designed for Alexa+, Great for bedrooms, dining rooms and offices, Charcoal
title: Sounds Great  But I Miss the Audio Jack
body: Ive owned several Echo Dots over the years, and the newest model definitely sounds better than the older ones. The audio is fuller, clearer, and surprisingly punchy for such a small speaker. For casual music, podcasts, and general Alexa use, it does exactly what its supposed to do. Setup is simple, Alexa responsiveness is solid, and it integrates easily into a smart home setup. As a compact speaker for a bedroom, office, or kitchen, its a nice upgrade in sound quality compared to earlier generations. That said  I really hate that Amazon removed the 3.5mm audio jack on the latest models. That was a genuinely useful feature. Being able to hardwire it into better speakers or an amp gave you flexibility. Now youre locked into Bluetooth or the internal speaker, which feels like a step backward for people who actually use their equipment beyond basic voice commands. If you just want a standalone smart speaker with better sound than older Dots, its a solid choice. But if you relied on the audio-out jack like I did, be aware its gone  and thats disappointing.

product_name: Amazon Echo Dot (newest model) - Vibrant sounding speaker, Designed for Alexa+, Great for bedrooms, dining rooms and offices, Charcoal
title: Another great smart home purchase, to say the least.
body: My previous Echo Dot, purchased back in 2018, was a third-generation. It was a white shuffleboard stone covered in cloth and it is amazing. I'll never say it's not; in fact it's so amazing that I passed it on to my mom and she enjoys it a great deal. So, my main motivation for purchasing this, the fifth generation issued in 2022  aside from familiarity with the brand  was I needed a Matter hub so my then-incoming Nanoleaf smart lightbulbs could work with my voice. But of course it is so much more. First thing I see is that the Echo Dot is no longer a white shuffleboard stone but is a smaller, navy blue sphere. Aside from being my favorite color, and thus very pleasing to look at, I find it that it is not practical any longer to mount on my wall because of its increased focus on the forward firing speaker; if I had mounted it, it would not be effective in spreading out to my entire room  the top of the speaker is reserved for the buttons which were present on the third-generation: volume up, volume down, action/wake and mute microphone, which leave no room for a speaker in that area. So it sits pretty on my side table facing the rest of my room, and does fine. First thing I hear is the sound quality is so much better than the third-generation Dot. The third-generation was okay in that regard, but it tended to sound a little overexposed when the volume was up high. Now, I don't find myself connecting via Bluetooth to my sound bar nearly as much because the speaker quality is just that good. The quiet parts of music are quiet and calming the way they should be, but the bass thumps quite effectively, surprisingly for such a small and lightweight speaker. Read more Read more

product_name: Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Plus (newest model) with AI-powered Fire TV Search, Wi-Fi 6, stream hundreds of thousands of movies and shows, free & live TV, find shows faster with Alexa+
title: Great Streamer, But Remote Needs Work
body: Ive been using the Firestick 4K for a while now and overall, I'm quite pleased with its performance and ease of use. The setup was a breeze, and I really appreciate the consistent user interface it provides across all my TVs, which is especially helpful for my wife. However, I have a serious issue with the remote. It feels cheap and flimsy, and honestly, the removal of the IR Blaster is baffling. I don't understand how this design made it to market, as it complicates setup for those of us with soundbars or surround systems that connect via optical cables. Additionally, the dedicated app buttons are a real annoyance. I often find myself accidentally launching other apps instead of streaming my preferred content on YouTube TV. I wish there were an option to disable them or even assign them to apps I actually use. If you're considering this device and can find it on sale for about $25, it's worth it for the streaming capabilities, but be prepared to navigate a frustrating remote experience.

title: Top quality audio after a few adjustments
body: This review is about the audio features of the JBL Vibe Beam earbuds. I'm an audio engineer so audio quality is very important to me. As such, I use wired connections as much as possible to maintain the integrity of the audio signal. However, many newer consumer-level products no longer have audio jacks, so I finally decided to invest in some wireless earbuds as a backup to the other earphones and headphones that I use. I didn't want to spend a lot of money as I didn't foresee myself using this product very much, and since this is my first foray into wireless audio earphones, if they were just ok or they broke after a while, then I wouldn't feel too bad about the little money that I spent. Also, I wanted to focus on finding wireless earbuds with excellent audio quality first as I already have an excellent high-end bluetooth earpiece for cell phone use, and I wouldn't be using this product as a bluetooth headset for my phone. After doing much research, I chose the JBL Vibe Beam's as they were at my price point, I'm familiar with JBL products, and I could reasonably expect that they were likely deliver the high audio quality that I've come to expect from JBL products. In my research, I read reviews that complained about the harshness of the high end with high frequency instruments like cymbals or the lack of bass, so I was a bit concerned. Read more Read more

title: Excellent product for the price
body: For the price point, these earbuds have been awesome!! Instead of getting overly expensive earbuds that I might lose or not like, I bought these as starter Bluetooth earbuds and haven't opted for anything else since. I mainly use them to watch shows or listen to music (especially when walking). I sometimes use them for phone calls. For me, the sound on calls is clear but I have gotten the feedback that it can sometimes be loud with background noise for the person on the other end of the line. I have small ears and the buds are a surprisingly comfortable fit. I did separately buy a silicone lanyard that I use every time with the earbuds so that I don't lose them. Without the lanyard, I am confident I would have lost the buds. The charging capacity is also great. The buds last a long time before needing to be charged. product_name: JBL Vibe Beam - True Wireless JBL Deep Bass Sound Earbuds, Bluetooth 5.2, Water & Dust Resistant, Hands-Free Call with VoiceAware, Up to 32 Hours of Battery Life (Black)
title: JBL Vibe Beam vs Soundcore Liberty 4 NC vs Soundcore P40i
body: I ordered 3 different earbuds all within a similar price range(+\- $30) to figure out which one I liked the most - Soundcore Liberty 4 NC, P40i, and JBL Vibe Beam. Here are my notes and comparisons. The Liberty 4 NC is my favorite but there is one aspect I dont like. They Libertys are very clear with good bass and their app allows you to customize EQ settings which is deal breaker for me. (All three pairs have custom EQs within their respective apps). The EQ settings and customization from the JBL app are far superior as they give you unlimited options. The JBL Vibe was less comfortable but more so than JBL Tune(ordered these a while back, the Tunes fall out constantly!). The JBL Vibes are not noise canceling and I knew that before hand so Im not knocking it. The JBLs are fine for the price point but not the best for quality as they use 8mm drivers vs 11mm drivers for the Soundcore. The one thing I dont like with the Libertys is the slightest white noise I get when between songs or silent parts. The P40is and Vibes dont have that problem. The P40i and JBLs have great bass and fit well but they lack in higher frequencies and thats important for audiophiles. The P40i also have a much friendlier app interface with many more preset features. BUT the Libertys have an option to set up your ideal listening EQ and that is a great feature as it adjusts to your preferred sound through samples and takes the guess work out. Read more Read more

body: Guess who I bought this for? Yes, the husband. Guess who did the laundry and forgot to empty the pockets and left these JBL ear buds in his jeans pockets? Yes the husband. title: Decent, but far from perfect (for me).
body: I've had these for nearly two months, using them at my job working with dogs. Typically, I listen to long-form videos on YouTube or music while working outside in a yard full of dogs. Having come from using some relatively basic ear buds bought at Target, I wanted something a little more durable and with stronger sound. I found these JBL buds for a decent price and decided to go for it, knowing that just about anything from JBL would sound better than what I had. All in all, they sounded great for the price and I enjoyed the easy touch controls for the most part, but there were some significant problems with how I was trying to use them on the job. For one thing, I'd never had ear buds in this design, with an Air Pod style handle or whatever and I worried that it would be unwieldy. Fortunately, it actually made getting them out of my ear or out of my pocket easier - so that was good. However, the touch controls were a little too sensitive for me, as I'd often find myself fumbling to grab one of the buds from a pocket and accidentally hit that touch spot in the process, either restarting my media or skipping onto something else entirely. body: I bought this to replace a tangle of cords cluttering my desk. With six AC outlets and three USB ports, it handles my laptop charger, monitor, lamp, speakers, and a couple of phone chargers all at once. The flat six foot plug and the included cable ties help keep the setup neat and the cord tucked away. The wide voltage design means I can use it in spaces with different outlets without worrying about compatibility. The six outlets stay accessible even with larger plugs in use, and the overall build feels solid enough for everyday use. One small caveat is that when all three USB ports are drawing power, the total charging speed can dip a bit, but I still get reliable everyday charging. Other than that minor note, the experience is consistently smooth and confident. Overall I am happy with this unit and would rate it five stars for keeping my devices safe and organized. product_name: Amazon Fire TV Stick 4K Max streaming device, with AI-powered Fire TV Search, supports Wi-Fi 6E, free & live TV without cable or satellite, find shows faster with Alexa+
title: Great device, but may not be for Plex users
body: For various reasons, I decided it was time to at least try a different streaming device, and I figured a FireTV Stick would be the obvious solution. I wanted a responsive device, and to try to ensure this, I figured I might as well go for the top model of the FireTV Stick, so I ordered the 4K Max. This device does not at all disappoint in that regard! It is very snappy while navigating through the UI and the various streaming apps I utilize, so I'm very pleased with that. It comes with a short HDMI extender, which is a great touch because it makes using a "stick" form device such as this not interfere with being in the way of your other HDMI ports. Make sure you use the power adapter it came with (or a USB adapter capable of outputting 2 Amps) because you will run in to problems quickly if it doesn't detect enough amperage. I rather wish it came with a longer cable, as it is not uncommon these days for a TV to be wall mounted, but that's just my own opinion. Setup is very easily done and has nice guidance every step of the way. Even getting the remote to work with my TV was super easy and included in the first time setup guidance. The remote ended up only being able to control my TV's power and volume, which is not a big deal, and I'm sure most individuals who buy this intend to use it as the main source of media to play on their TV. title: Perfect fit, fantastic value, great sound
body: I honestly love these. The ear seal/fit is much better than many of the other inexpensive true wireless earbuds I've used and the battery life is perfect for my gym sessions which last around 2-3 hours. They're also great for noise suppression when I mow grass. As far as sound quality goes, they're not going to beat high-end IEMs in a quite room, but they're better than or at least on par with earbuds from a certain influencer marketed brand and are a third the price. I've bought two pairs which I've used daily for close to two years. Unfortunately, on of the pairs died recently, one earbud just ran out of battery and putting it in the case to charge results in the case getting very hot, but the remaining earbud is technically still usable. I'm thinking of getting a third pair because of how much I like the fit though. Just to touch on a few recommended topics, I did try wearing them in the shower when I first got them but the water resulted in the volume getting cranked in one direction as a long press on the ear bud acts as a volume control. I actually have the same issue when sweat puddles on an ear bud. A quick pat dry resolves that issue though. It doesn't have active noise cancelling, of course, but the passive noise cancelling is very good. These are cordless/true wireless so no cord length issues. title: Great value for both phone calls and music
body: Sometimes I'm amazed by the advancements in wireless and micro technology, especially when it comes to audio. These popped up in an email from Amazon a few days ago. Though I usually ignore those Deal notices this one intrigued me. I have a JBL Everest over-ear headset that I love for indoor phone calls and they're pretty good for music too (though I don't use them that way). I often like to go outside when I'm on the phone and the JBLs aren't convenient to use with a stocking cap, so I'd thought briefly about getting something in-ear to wear under my hat. These were $35 and the reviews were good. I liked that the case wirelessly charges the ear buds, and the case can be charged wirelessly too. My Samsung phone has wireless charging so I already have two wireless chargers and they're very convenient (especially given that the USB port on my phone was getting sketchy). So I figured I'd give them a try. I played a variety of my favorite music. I first asked for Jeff Buckley live music and the first song that started playing as I stepped outside was "Hallelujah." I walked around the property listening to my favorite music and before I knew it I had been listening to music for about three hours. I had really only cared about the sound quality for phone calls and whether they would stay in my ears (which is usually a problem for me). They never came close to falling out and they are comfortable. For phone calls the sound quality is very good on both ends. And though they're rated as having a 33' range I walked all around the house and got considerably farther than that from my phone with two walls separating the devices and I had no dropouts. Overall I am so happy with them that after a week of using them I ordered a backup pair. Read more Read more

title: Good Wireless Earphones Option for Price
body: Works well compared to similar products. For around two years I was using a similar, different brand of wireless earphones also purchased off of Amazon, and they worked okay. I had originally switched over to wireless because my old headphone jack finally stopped working, unfortunately; now, of course, my new phone doesn't even have a headphone jack in the first place. I started buying these when I was no longer able to purchase from the previous brand off of Amazon. They were both around the same price and looked relatively similar. After a few months of using these, I can say that these are better than my previous brand, which I will refer as P.B. for short moving forward. As an avid music listener, these get the job done. For starters, they pair to my phone much faster (3 seconds max) as opposed to P.B. (7-12 seconds), they're also much better at keeping debris, junk, and other foreign materials out of the inner parts; I'd have to buy new earphones from P.B. every 3-5 months for this very reason. Perhaps my biggest compliment is the fact that the interactive "buttons" on the top sides of these earphones are far less sensitive than P.B. I'm personally not a huge fan of these buttons in the first place, but I can confidently say that I've accidentally pressed those sensors on these earphones relatively far less (maybe 2 or 3 times a week) then P.B. (at least 10 times a week). The case is also pretty durable; if I were to have dropped P.B.'s case with the earphones inside, there would have been a 100% chance that they would have gone flying; these, however, from the few times I've dropped the case, have stayed inside the case, undamaged. Battery life for both is about the same (about 4-5 hours but haven't really noticed a whole lot), as is/was audio quality. Overall a good product for the price; my only complaint is with the occasional, accidental, button press, pausing my music, which can't be disabled as far as a know. Frustrating, as this was a problem that I had with P.B. as well, but not a deal breaker. For casual music listing, this shouldn't be a problem. Originally had to return my first pair for issues with the hinge on the case breaking, but was sent a new product at no cost and haven't had any problems with the new one. 10/10 for price, 8/10 for audio quality, and 7.5/10 for battery life. body: Ive been using the JLab Go Air Pop+ for over a month now, and for the price, theyre incredibly reliable. Overall, an excellent 5-star buy for anyone looking for affordable, comfortable, and functional earbuds.

title: High Quality Video and Audio
body: The Blink Doorbell is quick and easy to set up. First, scan the QR code inside the Doorbell with your phone before physically setting it up. This will link it with your system, making theft of the Doorbell useless. Once activated, it will only work with your account-specific system. Placement: Optimal Height is generally 4048 inches from the ground. This is the typical height of a doorbell. You can buy an optional no-drill Blink Vinyl Siding Clip on Etsy to avoid putting holes in your siding. The clip slides under and between the siding. Intercom: to speak to someone through the Blink Outdoor 2K camera, open the live view in the phone app and then tap the microphone icon (unmute), at the screen bottom. The mic icon will turn green, allowing you to speak through your phone. Tap the mic icon again to mute, allowing the person at the Doorbell to speak. The audio on the Blink Doorbell operates like a walkie-talkie; only one person can speak at a time. There may be a 1-2 second delay. Night Vision: works fine, showing a clear view of the immediate surroundings. Also check that your phones Blink app has permission to use the microphone. Go to the phone settings > apps > blink > permissions > microphone > and select allowed. Thats all there is to it. This is an easy to set up, effective security device. Read more Read more

title: Clear images, local storage, with minor issues
body: We had been using a different brand video doorbell, but decided we didn't want to continue to pay an annual fee and have our videos stored in the cloud. This doorbell allows us to store up to 256gb of video in a flash drive in the sync module. It works well, but there are a few issues with it. It advertised that it can be hard wired which is true. However, that wiring only connects it to our physical doorbell ringer, allowing it to ring when the button is pushed. It still runs on batteries that need to be replaced eventually. Our last doorbell was hard wired with no batteries to worry about. There's also no way to set it up to work with a chime when it detects motion. It can use a Blink Mini camera as a chime, but that, or any other 3rd party chime, only work when the button is pushed. The only alert for motion is from the app. Our last doorbell had a chime that alerted us any time there was motion. When it does detect motion and we click on the notification to open the app, it shows a blank screen saying a recording is in progress. title: Good headphones for the price, some minor cons.
body: TLDR: good quality and sound for price, minor inconveniences in setup. ANC mode works well. These cause my ears to get very hot quickly, so if you have sensory issues this might be a dealbreaker. These headphones have good sound, especially for the relatively low price. The app is not user friendly, but not really required to use the item. The extremely tiny "user manual" included in the box is impossible to read if you are over 40, so you will need to scan the QR code to get directions online. All that being said, they are pretty easy to pair to IPhone. Also the noise cancellation is superb! My husband was sitting right next to me talking and I couldn't hear him at all with the headphones on in ANC mode. Battery seems good, I don't know for sure if these will auto shut off, but after a day or so of non-use you will need to re-connect in Bluetooth settings. Have used near water, so I can't address water resistance, but I bought them for an international flight soon, time will tell about durability as it's only been about a week so far.

title: Great general purpose headset - good for comfort, audio and battery life
body: This is the one to get, for general purpose Bluetooth headsets. The combination of bone conduction and speakers in front of your ears, not pointed into them, works great. My previous one was a single ear, in-ear type, which became annoying and uncomfortable. This one is comfortable and doesn't get in the way of environmental sound to my ear. The voice on the phone just appears in my head. It's great being able to work with my hands while I talk on the phone or listen to music, podcasts, etc. Music - I'm a sound engineer, and this is fine for casual music listening. You're not going to get deep bass or audiophile detail in music, and it's no substitute for good closed-back headphones, but it's mostly balanced and good enough. I use the Standard setting in the app. The Volume Boost setting doesn't seem to do much at all. The Classic setting, which is bone-conduction only and takes more battery power, doesn't sound as good. I tried other EQ settings, including the 5 band custom one, and I usually stay with Standard. If you need to use the bass or treble boost settings, there's probably something wrong with the source audio. Volume - music volume maxes out just a little before I wish it did. It's not super loud, but it's plenty in a quiet environment. Using it on a bicycle or with significant wind, you'd probably want to put a headband over it, and even then, it may not be loud enough. For podcasts or phone calls, volume is better; no issues with it not being loud enough yet. Read more Read more

body: The first thing I need to say is just get it!! This is such an awesome product and it is worth the price. Finally my doctor told me I fell into about 10% of the population that has this same problem, so the only solution for me was the over ear style. One last thing I need to mention is the sizing. Specifically the standard versus the mini. I'm a male, 5'11" and weigh 180lbs with basically a normal head and short hair. Based on some other reviews I got the mini and I'm glad I did. It's a perfect fit without too much play in the back of my head. I can easily move my head up, down and all around and the headphones stay in place with a great deal of comfort. product_name: Amazon Kindle Paperwhite Signature Edition 32GB (newest model) – 20% faster with auto-adjusting front light, wireless charging, and weeks of battery life – Metallic Black
title: Excellent clarity and blazing speed; a worthy upgrade.
body: The screen has excellent sharpness and contrast, the best of any ereader I've ever seen in person. It brings to mind another device that featured strikingly good sharpness and contrast despite the layers over the screen: The Voyage. I don't have one on hand to do a direct comparison, but it certainly seems to have that same sort of secret sauce going for it. I'll be very interested to see some direct comparisons from people who still have a Voyage on hand. It's not as apparent in photos, but I'm surprised at how much sharper it is than my 2021 PW. I knew from the early reviews that it would be better, but I wasn't expecting it to be so noticeable. Even my beloved Oasis comes up short in a direct comparison! Much has been made of the speed of this iteration of the PW, and it's not just empty hype. I never thought the word "fast" would ever really apply to an e-ink screen due to the limitations of the technology, but the Kindle team has made that a reality. Pages turn as quickly as I can tap the screen, and while I've never cried out to the heavens about page turns being too slow, it's impressive to see such responsiveness on an e-ink screen. The true benefits of this speed come into play when navigating menus, adjusting settings, and especially when typing. I honestly did not think it was possible for an e-ink screen to be so fast and responsive. This is truly impressive. The speed in itself is not a reason to upgrade to this model, but if you're already considering an upgrade, it is definitely an extra enticement. Read more Read more

title: Limitations beyond sideloading, but probably still the future.
body: Preface: I've used FireTV devices for quite a while now, and have learned to appreciate what separates them from their competition, but also some of the annoying quirks that they struggle with sometimes. This device, with VegaOS manages to do away with most of the quirks, but also takes away some of the reason why I buy FireTV devices in the first place. Hardware: I'll get this out of the way quickly, since it's basically unchanged and is perhaps the least-impactful aspect of any Fire TV device. Yes, it has a micro-USB port for power, get over it. Amazon probably has a warehouse full of them, and for a connector that you plug in once in a device's life... it's not really impactful. People will always fuss about the lack of USB-c, but they're missing the point. The remote is nicer than my previous remotes, seems more solidly built and feels better in the hand. Unsure how it'll last, but maybe it'll make less creaky noises after a year of service than previous remotes. Software: Read more Read more

title: Modern Power Hub With Smart Ports  Just Not for High-Draw Appliances
body: I really like this surge protector and ended up buying a second one after owning my first for a while  that alone says a lot about the quality and value for money. The layout is excellent: 8 outlets, plus 3 USB ports including a USB-C, which still feels refreshingly modern. The plug-in capability is great, and charging phones and accessories is fast and reliable with good charging speed. Its also very flexible in terms of versatility. I use it for everyday electronics, chargers, and desk setups, and the mountability makes it easy to keep things neat and off the floor if you want a cleaner setup. The only reason I didnt give this 5 stars is power handling. If something with a higher draw gets plugged in  like a hairdryer  the unit will shut itself off. On one hand, I appreciate the safety aspect, but on the other hand, it means this isnt ideal as a true all-purpose room surge protector for mixed appliances. Overall, its a well-designed, modern power strip thats perfect for electronics and charging stations  just be mindful of its limits with higher-power devices.

title: Def worth it
body: Perfect size & is very lightweight. I absolutely love physical books, but I dont have enough space for more in my home (plus packing all those books up for moving is a chore & very heavy). I purchased mine in the green color along with the matching fabric case & recommended charger (added up to around $175). It came about a month or so ago, & I havent been able to put it down since. The battery is incredibly reliable; the charge lasts FOREVER (realisticallyabout 5-7 days with daily use). I take it everywhere with me as it fits easily in my bag & the charge holds so well. As far as cost, I save so much money on physical books, primarily because of the kindle unlimited subscription which is roughly $12/month after the trial. I can read as many books as my heart desires for that price alone. *Mwah* Chefs kiss. The setup was a breeze, but the controls were a little difficult to figure out at first, especially on the swipe to get back to the home screen. But everything else is pretty easy. The brightness, text size & other settings can all be changed easily. If youre going back & forth on the ads vs. no ads feature like I did: CHOOSE ADS. Its more or less just book suggestions. title: I READ A BOOK IN 4 DAYS! I haven't read a complete book in years until I bought this Kindle!
body: I've been trying to read books again for the last couple of years without much success. In 2025 (and probably 2024), I never finished a single book that I started. That includes self-help, college textbooks, and friend's books! Fumbling with books, turning pages, trying to remember to carry it with me, all of that made it difficult for a working adult with a family to sit down and read, even for a few minutes. Not to mention my arthritis, needing to wear reading glasses, needing a light just bright enough to make out the words, and more. I ordered this Kindle on New Years Day and fully expected to get bored of it in a week and want to get my money back. I downloaded my first book (Included in KindleUnlimited), by an author I never heard of (even though she's critically acclaimed and has had books turned into movies) and started reading. I was INCREDIBLY SURPRISED how easy it was to read on this thing. It's MUCH better than a physical book, by leaps and bounds. I started reading the book on Monday and finished it by Thursday. I can't wait to start my next book! The book was good... and that kept me interested, however... a few things about the kindle made it easy to finish the book so quickly... 1) Mainly, it was how easy on the eyes and the size of the text. I didn't need my reading glasses at all and the text was clearer than any book I've opened in the last few years. Read more Read more

product_name: Mounting Dream TV Wall Mount for 32-65 Inch Television,Mount with Swivel and Tilt, Full Motion Bracket with Articulating Dual Arms, Fits 16inch Studs, Max VESA 400X400 mm, 99lbs, MD2380
title: Fantastic mount
body: This mount is excellent. It perfectly fits a standard 16 inch stud spacing, but no more than that. The outer most holes for the stud mount go out to only 16 1/4 inch, and have slotted holes for the smaller stud spacing. More specifically, I bought and mounted this for an LG G5 Series 55 OLED. Not only does it fit for this specific TV, but it also has room for adjusting it up and down, sliding left and right, and allows the TV to come out and inwards using the extendable hinges. I do like the little bubble level it comes with, its nice to keep and save in my office desk, for little nuance projects. Only thing I wish it came with, were 4 washers for the lag/stud bolts. The surface area that the bottom of the head of the bolt, doesnt seem to have much contact area for my liking. So I added 4, 1 inch wide washers to give the bolt more area to clamp the mount and spread the load across the slotted hole. Other than that, with a little measuring and thought, this mount was extremely easy to install. Do take the time to see how far up or down you want mount the long black mounts that actually attach to the TV. Other than that, its still holding up great. Before I mounted my TV, I grabbed a bathroom scale, and while i stood on it, I pulled more of my weight onto the mount, and I wanted to confirm it would hold 100 or so pounds. It did so very well, felt and sounded sturdy at about 140 pounds. This manufacture does state 100 pound limit if I can remember correctly. So just keep that in mind.

title: Awesome ear set -- can't be beat at this price point
body: Outstanding! For the low price (~$20), this is an amazing ear set. I have been using Apple Airpods and then SamSung Buds for the past 6 years. product_name: Soundcore by Anker P20i True Wireless Earbuds, 10mm Drivers with Big Bass, Bluetooth 5.3, 30H Long Playtime, Water-Resistant, 2 Mics for AI Clear Calls, 22 Preset EQs, Customization via App
title: best buds for the price. look no further.
body: I never write reviews, but I have to for this product. Just as most have said, these are AWESOME. I have had nothing but bad experiences with other wireless earbuds so I stayed away from them. But as my over the ear headphones of 8 years stopped working, I had to buy new headphones so I decided to give them another chance. I kept hearing good things about Soundcore so I trusted the reviews and they live up to the hype completely. The audio quality of course isn't the best, but it's clear enough, loud enough, and has enough fidelity not just for the pricepoint, but in my honest opinion exceeds it. But the most important thing of all, connectivity. That is the number one factor when looking for wireless earbuds. Many earbuds have decent quality, but if they don't stay connected, who cares. These always stay connected. Previous earbuds, the right would connect but not the left. This is because with other earbuds the right is the primary connected device, while the left is connected to the right bud. With these, it seems both buds are connected simultaneously. Also, these aren't just good for music and movies, even in games they don't lose their synchronicity. Only one problem, when I tried playing a PC game and watching a movie at the same time, the buds did cause significant lag and input delays in my game, but that is totally forgiveable. When the buds are dedicated just t your game and not to another app they work very decently. Most wireless earbuds I tried in the past always had this problem of synchronizing to games. I am absolutely blown away at the quality of these for the price. The biggest downside I have heard is they only last about a year before they stop being functional. But still, for only $20 these are unbeatable if you're on a budget. I recommend the a20i over the p20i as the bass is a little too loud, but you can easily fix this by downloading the soundcore app and choosing the "reduce bass" EQ setting. Also, these are bluetooth only. They don't utilize a USB dongle. So if you have an older laptop or desktop that doesn't have bluetooth, you will need to purchase either a bluetooth dongle or a bluetooth/wifi PCI-E card for your desktop. Read more Read more

body: I gave these ear pods three stars because I really havent had big issues with them - they are just a basic, mediocre set of ear buds in my opinion. These ones are too big and when I touch them to adjust them back into my ear, the touch control is initiated. All Ive noticed the touch control doing, though, is making beeps when I touch the earbuds to adjust them. Im not sure what else is supposed to happen, but I havent noticed anything changing when I touch the ear buds while Im listening to them. Its mostly just annoying. I also havent tried to swap out the rubber parts of the ear buds with smaller ones. I cant remember if this set even came with replacements, and I dont remember where mine are if they did come with them. So that may help the size issue if I were to find them.

title: First Mac, really impressed.
body: Honestly kind of blown away by how much I like this thing. This is my first Mac and it's insane how much power is crammed into such a small form factor. Not only that, but just overall build quality, fit and finish, everything just feels very premium. I've had several Windows laptops and they all just feel pretty cheap, even at this same price point. That being said, this is Apple's "middle ground" laptop for a reason. If you plan on doing a lot of video editing or intensive gaming, just get the Pro. But everything shy of that runs amazingly well on this, and in a much more compact form factor. You can honestly still play plenty of games on the Air. But if you want to play FPS or something like Cyber Punk, definitely get a Pro. Lite stuff like Balatro, MTG Arena, Hearthstone, Stardew Valley, etc run perfectly on the Air. Also, if you plan on this being your one and only computer, get the 15 inch, a Pro, or connect it to a monitor. For portability, 13 inch is king. But if you are going to be on this computer for 3+ hours a day, get the extra screen real estate.

product_name: Apple 2026 MacBook Neo 13-inch Laptop with A18 Pro chip: Built for AI and Apple Intelligence, Liquid Retina Display, 8GB Unified Memory, 256GB SSD Storage, 1080p FaceTime HD Camera; Indigo
title: Impressive web PC, first time mac user, massive upgrade over a 4 year old Lenovo Yoga 13
body: The neo is really impressive for $600. It has a nice screen, an incredibly rigid aluminum chassis, loads web pages and videos super fast, boots quick. Impressive Bluetooth and WiFi range and performance (Bluetooth is so much better then my old laptop), good speakers(for a laptop), good battery life, a decent keyboard, excellent trackpad, decent web cam, looks nice, low heat output and no vents to get blocked, compact and light. Highly recommended for anyone shopping for a compact web machine. The only negatives on the hardware are: lack of keyboard backlight, the interior edge of the chassis is sharp, not enough to cut you, but enough to be annoying when typing in some positions, not many ports just a normal USB C and a 2.0 speed USB C (both can be used for charging) and screen does not fold flat, so the laptop cannot be stood up against the wall. I was a bit unsure about the Neo because of it using a cellphone chip, but that was not a problem at all. The Neo is super snappy, as fast as my Workstation/Gaming PC when loading web pages and videos. Also I have not noticed any issues with the amount of RAM, 8GB seems sufficient for running a web browser, just as long as you do not go crazy on tabs in Chrome or if you use Firefox that actually manages tabs properly. As a web browser machine I doubt that this will have any performance issues. I am sure that you would hit limits of the mobile CPU if you do did something serious like video editing, but it is more then enough for more casual use. Read more Read more

Despite costing half what my i7 laptop did and having half the ram the processor is 60% faster at running Powder Toy than the i7. product_name: Beats Solo 4 - Wireless On-Ear Bluetooth Headphones, Up to 50-Hour Battery Life, Ultra-Lightweight Comfort, Powerful and Balanced Sound, Apple & Android Compatible - Matte Black
title: Pros and cons got me on the fence about keeping
body: So today is the last day before my return window closes and I am on the fence about keeping them. Straight up there are features I really like. But there are two major downsides that cause me to think twice. I have been wearing them everyday since the day I received them, for hours a day, in multiple scenarios : work, workout, yard work, housework, etc. Especially as I really wanted to give them a fair chance. Right from the get go just a few hours in they began hurting my ears. So I did what I usually do with other like-headphones: I turned them around. At first when I did this they seemed to be unidirectional but after a few times they became bidirectional and it really helps with the longevity of wearability, esp if I keep flipping all day on long days. But still nothing like my old headphones that I have had for years and paid $50 for, which have outlasted multiple other pairs. With my old ones I can go 8 hours before feeling the need to flip em around due to discomfort. But staying on the longevity side what I do love about them is the battery, which I can go three to four long days of wear without needing to recharge. Love love love that!! Esp when needing to run out of the house and didnt have time to charge my headphones. Pluss the ease of connecting to WiFi! Love!! Instead of having to go into systems and go thru the whole manual routine of connecting, these you just press the button and the screen pops up to connect! Plus they do let you know when you have a potential important message, whether text, email or fb and asks if you want it read. Then it seems to have smart capability to filter according to your interests. Even not to interrupt you again if you say no. One of the biggest drawbacks, though, esp when working in the heat, like yard work or cleaning out garage, is they cause my ears to sweat. And Im typically not a sweater! Hate that! And they really sweat! They are attractive and I got the pink, which as some have said dont look pink, except in certain lighting. Which honestly after using fur a month I do think of these as my pink headphones. If the ear cuff piece wasnt so small theyd be perfect! Sound quality and all that is good but Im not an expert on that, as Im typically listening to audio books or podcasts and not necessarily music. And they did come in a really nice packaging. But so today Im opting to wear my old headphones, which are finally, after ten years, starting to lose their charge-ability, but so thatll be the test of whether I just need to look for another version of those or decide to keep the Beats Solo. I do like them but like a pair of shoes, you gotta have comfortably! Read more Read more

product_name: Beats Solo 4 - Wireless On-Ear Bluetooth Headphones, Up to 50-Hour Battery Life, Ultra-Lightweight Comfort, Powerful and Balanced Sound, Apple & Android Compatible - Matte Black
title: Iconic style, modern performance
body: The Beats Solo 4 represents a major technical leap for one of the most recognizable pairs of headphones on the planet. While the iconic, foldable design remains familiar, the internal hardware has been completely overhauled to meet modern standards. The Gold and Black aesthetic provides a high-end, sophisticated look that feels more mature than earlier iterations. One of the most impressive upgrades is the staggering 50 hours of battery life, which allows most people to go an entire week without reaching for a charging cable. Even if you do run out of power, the Fast Fuel feature provides five hours of playback from just a ten-minute charge, and the addition of a 3.5mm port means you can still listen to music even when the battery is completely dead. Sound quality has also seen a significant refinement. These headphones now feature custom 40mm transducers designed to reduce electronic interference and distortion, resulting in a much cleaner and more balanced sound profile than the bass-heavy Beats of the past. They also support Personalized Spatial Audio with dynamic head tracking, which creates an immersive, theater-like experience for movies and music. It is worth noting that these are "on-ear" headphones, meaning they rest directly on your ears rather than surrounding them. While the new UltraPlush cushions are incredibly soft, they prioritize a secure fit that stays put during movement, which some users may find a bit snug during very long listening sessions. body: I purchased it as a replacement for the LG TV remote that I had for 2 years. On first use it was very easy to set up. Instant connection from the time that I put the batteries in. Voice controls did not work. I attempted the instructions both on the remote and Amazon, and these did not work. Neither do the Disney+, Alexa, or LG channel buttons however the Netflix and Prime Video buttons both work. The function as a magic remote was not worth the extra dollars. We will have to see if it lives longer than its predecessor. There are no instructions on how to set up the multicolored other Functional buttons, and therefore are essentially useless. Overall, the instant connectivity and thus minimal setup make it an excellent replacement remote. Im not sure that at double the cost of others that it is a worthwhile price for the nonfunctioning magic, remote and 4 of 10 nonfunctioning quick buttons.

title: Good Bulk Buy, but Not for All-Day Sitting or Active Jobs
body: I originally bought this 12-pack for my husband back in 2023 because the bulk price was hard to beat. For the first few months, they were a great budget-friendly staple, but after nearly two years of use, weve seen where they start to fall short. Durability & Wearability: Initially, these were soft and comfortable for daily wear. However, after several months of regular washing, the black color began to fade significantly, and the fabric started to lose its "new" feel. Waistband & Sturdiness: The waistband starts out firm, but over time, it loses its elasticity. It doesn't have the long-term sturdiness needed to keep its shape, which leads to the dreaded "rollability" issuethe waistband eventually starts to curl and roll down during movement. Support & "The Bunch": My husband is a heavy equipment operator, so he spends his day sitting and moving in a cab. After the first few months, the support just wasn't there anymore. He found that the legs would roll up and the fabric would bunch up uncomfortably while he was working. Absorbency: They are 100% cotton, so they are naturally absorbent and breathable, which is a plus for moisture-wicking. However, once the fit becomes loose and "baggy," that benefit is outweighed by the lack of support. Read more Read more

title: Nice enough product, but don't order your first pair online
body: The shoes themselves are comfortable, durable (so far), easy to clean, everything that you'd expect from the description. I went from a Crocs-hater to an appreciater. But don't order online until you're familiar with the sizing. The manufacturer doesn't offer half-sizes, Amazon recommends ordering a size down if you're in-between, and supposedly the shoes will stretch a bit with use... but in practice that did not work for us. If you have wide feet, or any issues with foot/toe swelling, it definitely makes sense to try the shoes on in-person first. Even wearing socks can make a big difference, because the rubber material is high-friction and doesn't allow "slipping in" easily. I eventually ended up with two pairs in different sizes, rotated seasonally based on weather (socks) and foot health. If you have a local sporting-goods chain beginning with "D" and rhyming with "Kicks", buy your first pair there, where you can try them on (and support the local employees)... then use Amazon for repeat purchases (since Amazon is currently about $15 cheaper). 5 stars for the product, 3 stars for ordering online.

title: Best bra Ive ever worn!
body: So, TMI. I am a 22 year breast cancer survivor that went through reconstructive surgery. After 22 years, the surgery has started to deteriorate. This means that I have one side that is a A cup and the other side is a C cup. It is almost impossible to find a bra or bathing suit that doesnt make me look or feel like a freak. I do have a silicone inserts for the smaller cup size, but it is very uncomfortable and hot. This bra is absolutely amazing. Even without underwire its incredibly supportive. It does not roll up or ride up like many bras do without underwire. It fits perfectly and the bra straps are wide enough that they dont keep slipping off of your shoulder. And best of all, I actually look normal when I wear a tighter shirt with it on. It is comfortable, soft, and frankly the best bra I have found so far. Even if you dont have the challenges that I do finding a bra, I think that you would be very pleased with this. My only problem with it is it has three hooks in the back. I hate the three hooks! So I simply hook it on and loosen the straps and just put it over my head. The plastic piece to tighten the straps is actually towards the front so its very easy to adjust once you have it on. All in all its a perfect bra!

product_name: LetsJoli Bras for Women Supportive Wireless Push Up Bra Full Coverage No Underwire Seamless Plus Size for Older Women
reviewer: Save a Life - Adopt A Pet
title: Great for large busts & injured shoulders
body: Have been "well-endowed" for 34 years since having my first child. These are the first & only bras I've found that not only feel great, but look great and hold "the girls" in a non-matronly, non-uniboob. position. I've injured both shoulders and have a long upper torso, and many bras come with straps that aren't quite long enough for me, and that rest right over previously repaired parts of my shoulder, causing pain with minutes or hours from pressure. Good support is crucial, as the pull on upper chest muscles & ligaments from inadequately supported breasts also causes me discomfort & pain. I've tried every style & type of bra & strap out there - x back, convertibles, strapless, wired & wireless, front close, sports bras, you name the brand & stule, I've tried it. Plus all the "extras" - strap extenders & pads, tape & silicome "cutlets", etc. Nothing that felt even halfway tolerable worked and still looked good under clothing except heavily padded ports or wired bras with stiff, shaped cups that made my breasts look even larger, and reminded of women's magazine ads in the 1960's for hard, pointy-cup bras. And, when menopausal hot flashes or hot, humid weather hit, those few tolerable bras became soaked, leaving me with swamp boob, rashes & carrying a spare dry bra whenever I left home. Prepping for my 3rd shoulder surgery, I decided to search for a few inexpensive non-underwire bras with straps that could be unhooked or completely removed, for wearing while trussed up like a turkey for a few weeks post-op. Wired ones are quite uncomfortable and rub my underarm raw when my arm is strapped against my side & immobilized for 4-6 weeks, and I needed something supportive that wouldn't rub or pull on the operated areas, and to keep me "decent" during visits for PT & dr appts post op. I found these, read reviews, and gave them a try. I take a 38-42 DD, depending on brand & style, in Olga, Bali, Maidenform, Glamorise, etc., and 1 side, after 3 shoulder surgeries, is less full on top & sags a bit lower due to ligament damage, so it doesn't quite fill out a DD cup without help, and that shoulder slopes a bit. I usually need to play with straps & add a little lift in that cup so my chest looks even & that strap doesn't slide off. Going by the LetsJoli sizing chart, I decided to order the 2XL. title: Expensive Look Without The Expense!
body: Sizing: If youre a true large like me and close to but less than fifteen pounds overweight, normal length and with a 44 chest, buy a medium and use the shirt to inspire you to lose the beer belly. If you have similar dimensions but are overweight by up to thirty pounds, get the large. Materials: The mediums are manufactured to 70% synthetic, 30% linenwhich is a great mix, as is doesnt have to be ironed after its washed and hung to dry, nor does it have to be ironed every time you get up from a chair. Ive had many 100% linen shirts and sure, they look great after you spend thirty minutes ironing them, just to look like a crumpled up newspaper after you get out of your car. My experience with linen shirts is what brought me here. Unboxing the shirt: I threw it on an ironing board the moment I unbagged it. I tried the Synthetic setting on the ironit wasnt enough. I then tried the Wool setting and set the steam setting to constant medium to high. It worked great. I didnt have to press down at all, just lightly glided the iron across wrinkled areas of the shirt. Within five minutes, the shirt looked like a hundred-dollar shirt. The material is light, yet feels heavy to the touch and breathes very well in the heat. The hidden buttons give a clean, modern look that allows the shirt to be worn with anything from shorts and sandals to dress slacks and shoes in a club. I like to wear these shirts, untucked with a pair of jeans and western boots. This shirt is very comfortable, fits nice and is extremely versatile. Read more Read more

title: I Love These Underwear and They are a Great Alternative to Expensive Ones!
body: Several months ago, I purchased some new cotton bikini underwear and prefer them over other, more revealing styles. I'm a mom and I've discovered I prefer comfort over style so cotton bikinis suit me really well! I saw these underwear and I'll admit, I was a bit skeptical because I wondered if they would be good quality or not. I have some items from the Amazon Basics line and like them so I figured I'd give these a try. The Amazon Essentials Cotton Bikini underwear come in a six-pack and you can currently choose from two styles: basic assorted and fashion assorted. The basic assorted package comes with the following colors: two white, one light pink, one light purple, one black, and one black and white stripe. The fashion assorted package contains: one salmon color, one navy, one navy and white stripe, one light purple, one white with black polka dots, and one white with black flowers. These underwear are made of 95% cotton and 5% elastane. You can machine wash in warm water and tumble dry low. These are made in Sri Lanka. I initially ordered the basic assorted package and liked them enough that I immediately ordered the fashion assorted package as well. The underwear are very soft and comfortable! I really like the variety of colors and I would buy more if they decided to provide more colors. I've washed these underwear several times now according to the instructions and I didn't notice any shrinking. The stitching looks like good quality and I love that they don't ride up, which means these will work nicely when I exercise! I usually wear yoga pants and notice slight panty lines but they are hardly noticeable if you aren't looking for them. The bikini style sits comfortably at my hip and the band at the waist measures 2 3/8 inches.
