product_name: Nippies Nipple Covers for Women - Reusable Sticky Silicone Pasties with Travel Case - Original Circle or Lift Style Available
title: Effective and Comfortable (especially for smaller busts)
body: First off I feel very weird writing a review for this product BUT its been such a game changer for me that, despite feeling awkward, I wanted to write a review. I have a ridiculously small chest, I wear a 32AA but if the size existed Id honestly fit better in a 28-30AA. Therefore I have an EXTREMELY hard time finding any real bra that fits me well and what does fit the best is sports bras. Unfortunately most sports bras dont really have enough padding to conceal the outlines of this part of the breast (unfortunately with being a very small bust size I feel as though it just makes it even more noticeable) especially with any semi-tight top and there is nothing I hate more than having it show even slightly. For most of my life Ive just avoided tops that are tighter but with a recent wardrobe change it became unavoidable and I needed some lighter browns which made the whole situation worse. So I decided to give this a try. I am so so so glad I did! It gives me enough coverage that even with a lightly padded sports bra I can still wear a more form fitting lighter colored top. They last all day (even in the height of summer and being outside in the sun all day and being fairly sweaty). Ive reused the same pair multiple times and the adhesive despite numerous times hand washing. To ensure a good firm stick I do take a witch hazel toner over the area to remove any lotion, Im not sure it is necessary to do that but I just want to make sure they stay put. I have very sensitive skin and despite the great grip it never bothers me! The only thing I kind of dislike is that if you sweat when you remove these there will be a dampness to the skin and covers BUT the dampness never ever leaks or shows and it never causes any kind of a rash or reaction on me. In fact the first time I used these was on a 90 day and after 6 hours outside and being hot dressed up in my Ren Faire clothes I was completely taken by surprise when I did remove them and discovered it was slightly damp, so you really cant tell. Im personally not a person that sweats much so my sweating in those conditions is much less than most people so perhaps someone else would have a different experience with these. The lack of sweating also means body odor really isnt something I really worry about (I mean I use deodorant because you should not because I actually need to and thats about the extent of my worry) but even with that dampness there was no odor. So for me that one things Im not fond of is totally endurable because this product allows me to wear so many more tops with less self-consciousness! I have no idea if these are better or the same as cheaper brands because this was the first I tried and it worked so well I dont plan on switching! I even swam with these once and they stayed on all day. I play volleyball, catch, frisbee, etc in the pool and dont often dunk and swim a ton so if you swam submerged for hours I have no idea if theyd stay but for my activity that includes some dunking these things didnt budge! I only got to try them once but this is going to allow me to wear a lot of bikini tops I would have avoided before! Seriously, life changing! Read more Read more

product_name: Nippies Nipple Covers for Women - Reusable Sticky Silicone Pasties with Travel Case - Original Circle or Lift Style Available
title: UPDATED 4/2/12: One of the best purchases I've ever made.
body: I'm a small B-cup and don't really need a bra, and find bras uncomfortable. So, I bought my first pair of adhesive Nippies back in January. I have worn them nearly every day since then, and for me, they have been about as perfect as a product could be. Comfort: They're extremely comfortable -- so comfortable, in fact, that I occasionally forget to take them off before going to sleep because I forget they're there. I was afraid that the adhesive might irritate my skin, but it hasn't at all. They stay on very well and are easy to wash after use (I use plain old soap and water). Coverage: The coverage they provide is remarkably good, given how extremely thin they are. I can wear a form-fitting white tee-shirt with just the Nippies underneath, and my nipples aren't visible. I recently tried one other brand of nipple covers (Spicy Spot) because they were less expensive, and although they were thicker than Nippies, they were almost completely transparent and my nipples were quite visible under light-colored shirts. So in terms of how well they do their job, Nippies have definitely been worth the money for me. I love not having to worry about bra straps showing! Lifespan: As I said, I wore my first pair almost every day for 3+ months, so my estimate is that they've lasted about 100 wears. They have just now gotten to the point where enough of the adhesive has worn away that I can't wear them with looser shirts (though they're still fine with more form-fitting shirts, which hold them in place). title: great product but read the fine print
body: I got these to try out because I recently purchased some new bras that are not padded; just thinly lined. I had hoped with this horrid summer heat and humidity the unpadded bras would not get so damp from me sweating. The problem with the unlined bras is nipple show through so, after reading about various products in this category, I tried these. They are simple to apply and come in a rather sturdy flip-top case that can be used to keep them clean and in shape. Upon arrival they have tissue-like paper against the adhesive part and each is set on top of a thin plastic domed form for keeping them separated and in proper shape. To apply, I peeled away the tissue paper, placed the product adhesive side up in the center of my palm, leaned over and applied it, holding it in place a second or two as instructed so body heat helps adhere the adhesive to skin. It was smooth and flat without any wrinkles or puckers. That was at 4am this morning when I got dressed. It was pretty much invisible and I must admit, once I put my bra in place I could not tell I was wearing them. I could tell, however, that I had no peek-through or poke-through of my bra or my thin summer tank top. That is until I stopped in the grocery store and got to the frig/freezer sections. By the time I got out of that section of the grocery store, nothing short of a patio brick would have stopped my show through so if that is the reason you're looking at these, I'm not sure they will be the cover you desire. title: Great fit and comfort at an affordable price
body: After losing a significant amount of weight, I found it impossible to find a bra that fit. I bought many bras that have ended up un-worn in the back of a drawer. I am happy to have finally found a bra that fits and is comfortable. I ordered size small. The material and banding feel great. All parts of the bra stay in place. Nothing shifts around and the straps don't fall down. Snap extension are provided, if needed. The wide bands help smooth the underarm and back area. These bras are lightly padded, and I do not like bras that don't have the padding completely stitched down all the way around because the padding bunches up in the wash and is difficult or impossible to straighten out. The only drawback to these bras is that the padding isn't stitched all the way around. After one hand-washing (as recommended), the padding stayed in place. Hand-washing is inconvenient so I may try to machine wash one of the 3 I bought to see if the padding remains in place. At any rate, if I have to completely hand-stitch the padding in myself, it will be worth it for the comfort and sizing these bras provide. The pricing is good for the quality of these bras. I will definitely be buying more. I wish they came in a dark brown color.

title: Small band/large breasted women lose again (too big and too small at the same time)
body: So bummed. This bra was super comfy, BUT  the band was too big, and the cups too small. I was on the last clasp available, and still wished there was another row or two to further tighten it. But at the same time, I was spilling out of the tops of the cup. If I rearranged I could get them un-spilled at the top, there was spillage in the underarm area (women, you know what I mean by this). Granted, Im a busty gal. I wear a 36 H, sometimes a G, and sometimes an I  it depends on the bra. But I read everything I could on this bra - and read a lot of reviews too - so I thought I was making a good choice with my size, but  nope. I considered trying a replacement by going up a size, but if the cups fit on a larger one, surely, the band would be even bigger as well  and I was already wishing for a tighter band. So, return it I must. Bummer drag. I loved the feel of it, and it DID provide lift; I could also see that it would have given a nice shape if only there had been a teeny bit more room in the cups. The material was buttery and soft and I really liked it; Im sad to have to return it. We small backed but large breasted women lose again.

Sellers must offer one of the following: A return address within Canada where you can ship the item A prepaid return label; or A full refund without requesting the item be returned For more information about returns to sellers, please refer to Returns to Third-Party Sellers. And, according to other purchasers, the cost to return was more than the cost of the item. So, basically a rip off.

product_name: Comfort Colors Adult Heavyweight Short Sleeve Tee, Style G1717/G1745, Multipack
title: Comfort Colors T-shirt sizing is inconsistent from shirt to shirt
body: Do a search in the reviews of these T-shirts using the word inconsistent and youll see others have similar comments to mine. With almost 27,000 reviews on these shirts, not sure if anyone will even see this review, but here we go. I have about 5 Comfort Colors T-shirts that I got in the past year; all are size Large, and they fit great in the body and neck with nice soft cotton, and they are fantastic T-shirts. I recently ruined a denim-colored Comfort Colors T-shirt using Spray-n-Wash on a couple food stains on the chest; the Spray-n-Wash faded the blue color in the treated areas. No worries, Ill just order a replacement shirt. I decided to order two replacement Comfort Colors T-shirts with one being blue jean-colored and the second being a denim-colored T-shirt. Both are blue with blue jean being slightly lighter blue than the denim, but I decided to order two since I like these T-shirts so much. The blue jean shirt arrived and fit great and is just like all my other large Comfort Colors shirts, and its a keeper. However, the denim shirt I received was too small: it was tight in the chest, the body was shorter, and the sleeves were shorter. I have attached two photos with one showing this new denim shirt on top of the shirt I ruined with Spray-n-Wash and another showing this new denim shirt on top of an orange Custom Colors shirt to show the smaller size of the new shirt. The neck-size in the new denim shirt I received was fine, but the shirt was noticeably too small in all other dimensions. I immediately ordered another denim-colored shirt hoping to get a correct size shirt, and the second denim shirt arrived the next day, and it was also smaller in all the dimensions exactly like the first denim shirt I had just ordered. All three of these new Comfort Colors shirts (one blue jean and two denim) and the denim shirt I ruined with Spray-n-Wash were sold and delivered by Amazon (i.e. none were third party vendors). Im returning both of the too-small denim-colored shirts, and Ill live without that color and enjoy my new blue jean shirt. Read more Read more

product_name: Rustler Men's Classic Relaxed Fit
title: Why I only buy Rustler, Wrangler and Lee.
body: Why I only buy Rustler and Lee (Protect your pride and your comfort) If you want to know why most modern jeans feel like trash, its the fabric. Take a look at Gap, Levis, and the other big "fashion" brands. Most of them aren't even 100% cotton anymoretheyre full of plastic and "stretch" fibers. Real men don't need plastic in their pants. Those cheap brands also use a Right-Hand Twill weave (running bottom-left to top-right). Over time, that weave forces the jeans to twist, making the left leg tighter and mushing your GONADS into your leg. Its annoying, its uncomfortable, and its weak. If you value your testosterone and your comfort, stick to Lee, Rustler, and Wrangler. Rustler and Wrangler use a Broken Twill weave. Its a heavy-duty, 15oz, 100% cotton denim that actually molds to your body instead of fighting it. Because the weave is balanced, it won't twist and crush your anatomy. Lee uses a high-quality Left-Hand Twill that stays soft and straight without that cheap shrinking and leg-twist you get from the other guys. Do your body and Western Civilization a favor: make peace with your GONADS, be a man, and buy a pair of heavy-duty Rustlers. High-quality jeans for a price that doesn't break the bank. title: My new favorite no show socks
body: I have very high arches which means most no show socks dont stay on my heels. Plus I where a size 5 and most socks are for size 5-10 which results in extra material that rubs uncomfortably against my toes. I had a list of features I looked for: bamboo or organic cotton content, not super thin but not thick, high enough on the heel they would stay in place but low enough they wouldnt show when wearing loafers or flats. The Wernies socks check every box. They fit my feet like they were made for them. They dont squeeze my toes and hug my arches well. The seam across the toes is positioned well so it doesnt chafe. Theres a special stretch band around the heel that keeps the socks in place even with my joggers. They do not show when wearing my loafers and flats. I have washed them a dozen times now in cold water on the gentle cycle and tumble on low heat. Washing the socks has not changed the fit or feel. I love them enough I ordered another set. If I could Id give them an extra star just because they dont come connected by those plastic pieces that either snag your socks or leave a hole. The socks come in a large flat package.

product_name: Wrangler Authentics Men's Classic Cargo Stretch Short
title: Comfortable and convenient
body: Got these to replace some old cargo shorts that aren't made anymore and are developing holes. So far I have been very pleased. The waist fits true to size and the fit is relaxed through the seat and thigh, which is exactly what I was looking for since I have somewhat stout thighs relative to my waist. I'm 6'0", about 200lbs, with a 34-35" waist. The material feels sturdy without being overly heavy, about as thick as old ripstop BDUs. Front slash pockets are generously deep; no problem carrying a 5.5" smartphone in there. Rear pockets are sufficiently deep and are narrow enough that a trifold wallet won't turn itself sideways. Cargo pockets are adequately sized and have hook and loop closures at the corners. The "tech" pocket behind the left cargo pocket is very convenient for me, as I'm used to carrying my phone on that side. I can slide my Samsung Galaxy S25 in there and it fits well without worrying about it falling out. I wore these for a couple of days at Disney World last week and they performed very well. Moving my phone to that tech pocket made rides with lap bars or belts much more comfortable. I even washed these shorts at the resort so I could wear them on the long drive home. If I could change anything about them, I'd add about an inch of length, as I prefer a full 11" inseam on cargo shorts. And maybe add a button or some kind of closure to the rear pockets. But aside from that, these have been pretty close to perfect. I expect I'll order more as I replace more of my older cargo shorts. Read more Read more

body: Nice and simple flip-flop. I'll wear these around the house, the yard, and outside. They're what I call "multi-tasking" flip-flops. The reason this isnt a full five-star rating is that the plastic body, while functional, feels cheap rather than premium. It works exactly as intended, but it doesnt have a high-end build feel. For practical travel use and peace of mind at a reasonable price, its a very useful addition to any travel kit.

title: Great fit for a 6'4" dad bod  comfy, lightweight, and deceptive enough to look formal.
body: Im 6'4", about 250 lbs with a classic dad bod, and the 38x32 fit me perfectly. Waist and length are spot on. If youre built anything like me (not exactly tour pro lean), these will probably work really well for you. Fit & Comfort: These are very comfortable, especially for long days. Ive worn them for full rounds and all-day wear, and the stretch is in that sweet spot  enough give to move, bend, and swing without feeling sloppy. They are lightweight and breathable, which is huge when youre walking in heat or just standing around on a warm day. I've also worn them to work, and nobody knows I'm wearing stretchy pants. The only downside for me is that the fabric can cling to my legs a bit, especially around the lower legs (almost a static cling type of thing). Its not a dealbreaker, but if you hate any kind of cling, just know that going in. Look & Versatility: They look better than I expected at this price point. Easily pass for business casual with a polo or button-down, so I can go from course to casual dinner without looking like Im in gym shorts. Clean lines, good shape, and they dont scream cheap golf pants. Read more Read more

body: I ordered four of these tops in different colors when Amazon was having a sale. I wasn't sure if a medium or large would fit best, so I purchased two medium and two large. If you prefer a more relaxed look, sizing up gives you a little extra room without being bulky or sloppy looking. The material is very soft and a decent weight where it won't be see through, but light enough to stay cool. It doesn't look like it will have issues with pilling. I haven't worn it in hot, humid weather yet, but I'm really hoping they breathe and are comfy then because they're so beautiful I'd love to order a few more! The color selection is fantastic--it's not often you find a shirt that has so many options for colors and they're all pretty, it was tough to choose! The neckline is so flattering as is the cut and fit of the rest of the shirt. The length is great to leave out or tuck in if you prefer and it can be dressed up or down. A great buy for a summer staple!

title: Reliable fashion tape
body: Removing the tape can be a little uncomfortable  it felt a bit like pulling off a band-aid (though the discomfort was brief). Because the hold is strong, if your skin is sensitive or you have a lot of movement (dancing, brisk walking, etc.), Id recommend doing a small patch test first  on a less visible area  to make sure youre okay with the adhesive. For some very delicate fabrics (like thin silk or lace), you might need to be cautious  I avoided heavy pulling or repositioning once the tape was on. If you want a simple, effective fashion solution for dresses, low-cut tops, strapless outfits, or just to prevent wardrobe malfunctions, this tape is worth owning. Its not perfect  the removal isnt completely painless  but the convenience and peace of mind it offers make it a solid 4-star product in my book. Id recommend it to anyone who wants reliable stay-in-place support without bulky undergarments.

title: Great hold, not not without consequences
body: I really wanted to give this product 5 stars because the hold is incredible, unfortunately, the after effects force me to give it 3 stars. Ive used this tape twice, both times with the same outfit: the By the Lake Padm Amidala dress. The first time was on May the 4th at Disney, worn for about 5 hours. It held perfectly, even in the Florida heat, and I had zero issues. The second time, I wore it for nearly 12 hours. Once again, the hold itself was flawless, nothing shifted or moved. However, after several hours I began feeling pain and irritation where the tape was applied. When it came time to remove the dress, I had to be extremely careful because of how strongly the tape adhered to my skin. Despite being cautious, the tape peeled off skin in multiple areas, and I later developed very painful warts in those same spots. This was especially surprising because I do not have sensitive skin. My takeaway: this tape works very well, but Id urge people to use it with caution. Avoid wearing it for long periods, and be especially careful if you have sensitive skin. If you do wear it for an extended time, be prepared for possible skin damage when removing it. Read more Read more

product_name: Hanes Men's Beefy-t T-Shirt, Heavyweight Cotton Tee, 1 Or 2 Pack, Big & Tall
title: Good Quality But Sleeves Are An Issue
body: I'm giving this shirt 4 stars for one reason: the sleeves. I'm not sure why big and tall sizes have sleeve seams that go down so far past the shoulder and have a length that goes past the elbow. It's like you're wearing a tent. Why not make the chest and belly area bigger, which they do, but make the sleeves more proportionate? Not everyone who wears a 4XL is elephant-sized with long gorilla arms. I'd wager most aren't. With that said, the quality of the t-shirt is nice, and it's comfortable. It's a thicker cotton that held up well after a wash and didn't shrink too much. It fits well and is longer than a typical 4XL. I haven't noticed any loose threads or discoloration. It's well-made, and, given the price, is hard to complain about, despite my aforementioned gripe. I'm using these as blanks to press my own designs, so even if the sleeves are aggravating, it's cheaper to make your own clothes to wear than to spend $40 for a single graphic t-shirt at a big and tall store. If you can look past the sleeve issue, these t-shirts might be a good fit for you.

product_name: CRZ YOGA Butterluxe Yoga Leggings 25" - High Waisted Buttery Soft Womens Workout Lounge Pants
title: Amazing Leggings! But when buying leggings for practice, you get what you pay for :)
body: First of all, let me say, these leggings are AMAZING: they're comfortable, fit perfectly, the material is as described - literally buttery - and they look incredible. This color is also gorgeous, it took a while for my size to restock just to get this color and it was worth the wait. HOWEVER, these being my first pair or CRZ leggings, I am a bit disappointed. I practiced in them 3 times and they seem really solid for moderate to low impact practice, especially Hot. They're very breathable. (I'm talking yoga here, but they'd also be very good for gym or running). Here's the CATCH: I wore them last weekend under a pair of sweats while hiking. I'm not sure how or when it happened, but after I got home, I noticed a huge hole just below the butt in the back center of my right leg. It looked a lot like a nylon tear or rip, rather than a tiny hole that you'd see with more durable, expensive fabrics. I say all this to say that when it comes to clothing whose durability you rely on, you really get what you pay for. I.E. My first pair of Lulu leggings were a pair of black Aligns and I wore them 2 years straight, never once ripping, snagging, no holes or otherwise, and I just regifted them because they still look nearly brand new. I am glad to know how CRZ's hold up now so I can use them for less rugged activity. Regardless, I still love them and I will still be purchasing this brand. I also won't be throwing them out, I've worn them a total of 4x, so I WILL find a way to layer or repair. Read more Read more

title: Great for big tummies
body: A friend recommended these and Im obsessed now. Just ordered another pair after only having these for a week. Theyre so silky and smooth. No idea what pants size I am right now (16-20?) but Im 195 pounds, big tummy (I look like I could be pregnant), big thighs, big calves, and big muffin top. The amount of space my stomach takes up when Im sitting down vs standing is really different, so Ive only been doing stretchy waistband clothes the last few years since trousers and jeans either dig in when I sit down or fall down when Im walking. These are perfect. I only do high waisted and these are very high waisted. They dont feel too tight at all, yet they do feel like they hug and support me. The top part has only folded a tiny bit once, when I had just gotten in the car. But around the house they DONT slide down at all, and dont normally fold on me. Theyre not hard to get on and off. I wear thongs since the seam of normal underwear can be seen, and unless I do a laser cut or no seam thong, I can see the faint outline of the top still, so I personally dont wear these leggings with very cropped things. I also do darker colors because I am a little self conscious about how lighter colors dont hide my extra contours with the shine of leggings as much as the darker colors, but I feel actually sexy and athletic in these. Just wearing these around the house gives me a confidence boost, and also a boost of encouragement to go to the gym. Im not self conscious of my booty but my booty does look extra great in this lol. title: Great for Cold & Iced Liquids
body: 4 stars  great for cold and especially iced liquids; poor for hot. No spillage at all. Would buy again. I recently tested a variety of tumblers with a digital thermometer, in a variety of conditions. Two important findings: (1) The best predictor of temperature after 3 or 24 hours was the tumbler's volume, not the brand or design  if you can stuff it with more ice, it will stay colder, longer. (2) The differences were too small to matter, in my opinion. After 3 hours, there was barely a 1-2F among the tumblers. Even after 24 hours, the spread was under 6F. Each retained Ice & Heat for long enough periods that I could prepare the night before. Accordingly, I chose based on design, ease of cleaning, and flavor. I was surprised that the Owala ended up the best for sipping cold liquids. Despite having a tiny opening to drink from, it never gets blocked by ice (crushed, cubed, or otherwise). It also hasn't gotten blocked by boba or passion fruit seeds or anything else I've had in the cup. Read more Read more

body: Just received this mug/tumbler, and Im impressed enough with it to want to mention a few things about it. Pro: ~The first impression is that this is a quality product, solid and hefty (okay, the latter might be a small con for some; at about 10-1/2 oz empty, it does weigh more than youd expect for its size). ~The top screws on very smoothly, much more so than my long-time favorite Contigo tumbler whose more rubbery top takes a little more effort to screw on. ~The design of the sipping spout makes it surprisingly comfortable to put ones lips around. ~The fact that its spill-proof, although being the prime reason I bought this, is still worthy of mention. As much as I liked the similar-sized Corksicle wine tumbler, two spills on my clothes while carrying it prompted me to search for and find this Owala. ~Its matte texture is both nice-looking and feeling. title: Perfect last minute table runner!
body: I used these for a last minute table runner for Easter dinner. I twisted the fabric down the middle of the table, spreading open every few inches to place small centerpieces and Easter eggs. I placed another runner on both sides of the center runner and added place settings on top (for a total of 10 place settings). Took some stretching because I made random loose twists around each plate. Sorry I forgot to take a final photo- the one shown is when I first started playing around with just one piece of the material, as I had no idea how I was going to use it. I also purchased the matching napkins which turned out to be a nice unusual twist - I placed one on each plate and placed the silverware on top. So simple & easy and pretty. Perfect dusty rose color. Nice fabric - just the right amount of thin while not being flimsy. Packing was creative and protective while also not using unnecessary packaging. Highly recommend. Hope this helps someone!

product_name: Fullstar The Original Pro Chopper - Vegetable Chopper and Spiralizer, Food Chopper with Container, Fruit Slicer, Kitchen Gadgets & Gifts, Home Essentials (4 in 1, White)
title: 3 years later, still chopping like new!
body: I bought this in May of 2023 and as of February of 2026, this little chopper is still going strong! It's super easy to use, makes chopping anything super fast, and the blades have stayed sharp as anything. I only use the two square blades, so I can't speak on the others, but if they are of the same quality, then I assume they would be great too. It cleans super well in the dishwasher as long as you brush out any bits of food in the top grid and on the blades first. Otherwise they might not wash off and end up dried to the blades, making it much harder to clean. I've had to clean the dried bits off more than once when my kid loaded the dishwasher and didn't make sure there were no food chunks on it. I especially love this thing for chopping onions. Thanks to the pieces being contained in the bin after being chopped, they don't have the opportunity to make my eyes water. I leave it all in the bin until it's time to dump it in the pan. I have recommended this thing to everyone I know, plus in posts on Reddit. It's probably my most used kitchen gadget, getting used almost every single time I cook. Read more Read more

product_name: Fullstar The Original Pro Chopper - Vegetable Chopper and Spiralizer, Food Chopper with Container, Fruit Slicer, Kitchen Gadgets & Gifts, Home Essentials (4 in 1, White)
title: Great for Certain Veggies but the Cleanup is a Chore
body: I purchased this vegetable chopper in mid-2023 to make meal prep more efficient and slightly more fun, but after over a year, my feelings are mixed. The assembly is straightforward as the different blade options snap and lock into the base easily, and the overall fit of the components is secure. It has a clean, professional appearance on the counter, but the actual functionality depends entirely on what you are trying to chop. While the machine offers versatility with several blade options, it doesn't meet the bar for everything in my kitchen. Soft vegetables like tomatoes are not good candidates as they tend to get smashed, and very hard vegetables like carrots are too difficult to push through. For me, it really only works well for bell peppers and onions, and even then, you have to cut the vegetables down significantly first unless you want to throw your entire body weight into it. The biggest drawback for me is the cleanup. The blades are not the easiest to clean, and even using the provided plastic claw, removing debris is rarely effortless. Since I only regularly use it for two types of vegetables and then freeze them in batches, I don't feel it provides great value for the money for my specific household. However, I can see where someone who chops a much wider variety of moderate-density produce daily would get more use out of this. Read more Read more

product_name: ThermoPro Digital Instant Read Meat Thermometer for Grilling Waterproof Kitchen Cooking Food Thermometer with Ambidextrous Backlit for BBQ Grill Smoker Oil Fry Candy Thermometer
title: Acceptable for the Price
body: It seems absurd to spend $50, or even $100, on a meat thermometer. But if you want a good thermometer that works quickly and accurately, then the price is just a bullet you'll have to bite. I tested four meat thermometers: this one, the Thermopro Lightning ($45), the Thermopro TP620 ($30), the Thermopro TP19H ($15), and the Thermoworks ThermoPop 2 ($47). Some meat thermometers have some lag between the first displayed reading and the final accurate reading. With the TP19H, this lag is the most pronounced, as it takes between 10 and 20 seconds in many cases to reach an accurate reading. The TP620 and the ThermoPop 2, have less lag, but still take between 5 and 10 seconds to reach an accurate reading. The Thermopro Lightning, however, is a true instant-read thermometer, as it provides a fast initial reading, and the final accurate reading takes no more than two seconds to appear. The Lightning is the only one that I know that I can trust to quickly provide an accurate reading. I find this to be surprising given Thermoworks' reputation, but perhaps their Thermapen ONE, which I did not retest since it retails for $125, is what merits their reputation. For $45, however, the Thermoprop Lightning appears to be mightly close in performance to the much more expensive Thermoworks and is certainly better than the ThermoPop 2 that I tested. The ThermoPop doesn't even have a magnet or hook. All of these thermometers are waterproof under running water. I probably wouldn't put them in the dishwasher, however. The displays on all of these are fine, and I think that the Thermopro Lightning's display stands out quite well. If you're looking for a quality meat thermometer that will do exactly what you expect it to do, get the Lightning. But if you need the cheapest meat thermometer out there, the TP19H here will suffice. But be warned: the lag of the thermometer in providing an accurate reading will likely be frustrating and uncomfortable, especially over substantial heat, which is obviously the circumstance in which you're likely to be using this product. I've used all of these products, and I didn't want to spend $45 on a meat thermometer. At $45, the Thermopro Lightning isn't cheap, but if you can swing it, you'll have a for better meat thermometer that truly provides three times the value compared to this cheaper model. title: Well made, easy to clean, and no leaks.
body: Its very good. Keeps ice for at least 18 hours. Stays super cold, doesnt leak, easy to clean. Works great for hot liquids too but I would be worried about the straw as I become more aware of microplastics and begin to eliminate them from my life they are literally everywhere if you actually analyze what they are in and how the break down over time we have a really tough uphill climb. I would like to see this straw replaced with something that will never break down and get into our system period. We would ideally have to use only a product with steel insulated walls and no plastic contact, not even the o-ring. Even those break down and get into our liquids over time and that amount of time is very short. It doesnt fit in all cup holders because of the cup holder depth, if its a deep cup holder then the handle gets in the way. Its versatile to an extent I guess, but it becomes cumbersome quickly depending on the application. I think its good for hangout at the pool or beach but beyond that its size and shape start to annoy pretty quickly. Its also heavy if youre loading with ice and liquid and if you add chopped fruit or veggies then youve got yourself a pretty weighty jug rapidly. Its also very tall because they try and accommodate standard cup holders so keep that in mind, its easy to knock over and if its full of ice, liquid and chopped fruit and veggies for flavor enhancing its quickly like carrying a small carryon or cinder block with a handle. Overall though its a tank, keeps liquids cold as heck, doesnt leak, sturdy, and easy to clean but quickly can feel like youre carrying a 5lb infant around, thats top heavy, not stable in many standard cupholders becoming a cumbersome dumbbell quickly. title: Great little product! Hard-boiled eggs are no longer a challenge!
body: Bought this about two years ago because the egg-timer I had that actually sits in the boiling water with the eggs wasn't working as consistently as I had hoped. I saw this Dash Rapid egg Cooker and decided to give it a try. Love it! Mostly the eggs are cooked to satisfactory doneness, although occasionally a smidge under (but not grossly) or a tad over, but definitely cooked and edible. I found that using the 4+ level on the measuring cup for 6 eggs and filling it to just above that mark is enough water to cook the eggs to the required doneness. I've used the omelet feature a couple of times and it's okay, but mainly I use the DASH Rapid Egg Cooker for making hard-boiled eggs. My husband loves hard-boiled eggs so this was a great way to make sure he had his weekly quota. I would recommend this product! I've read other reviews where the purchaser said there's no way to store the trays and other paraphernalia, which is not exactly true. The trays (there are three; one for boiled eggs, one for scrambled, and a split tray for maybe omelets) fit conveniently inside the unit, with the stem and measuring/poking cup nestled in the split tray. I've not had any issues with them not fitting. And there is just one way to use the stem (I rarely use it, though) and it fits just one way, too. Doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out (and I am not a rocket scientist!). If I can figure it out, anyone else can, too. :-D Read more Read more

product_name: Dash Rapid Egg Cooker - Boiler, Poacher & Steamer - Includes Measuring & Recipe Guide - Dishwasher Safe - BPA Free - Holds 7 Eggs, Black
title: Simple, inexpensive Egg Cooker makes perfect soft-boiled/hardboiled eggs!!!!!
body: I love poached eggs but using 2 or 3 cups of water for 2 eggs and waiting for the water to boil was annoying, not to mention having to clean a teflon-free pan with egg glomming onto the sides.... until I found this little thing. I don't actually use the little plastic poaching pan that comes with this, I just stand them up on the grid and steam them. Then I do like the Brits do, whack off the top and either eat it right out of shell or spoon it on to a plate. Perfect every time! Uses a couple of tablespoons of water compared to a whole pan and cooks in about 5 minutes cutting my consumption of electricity and water! I And no cleaning egg residue out of the saucepan! I concede I'm not as handy as the Brits whacking off the top but bought an egg cracker that does the job quite well. And it would be a good idea to have at least one egg cup to hold the egg while you open it because it's VERY hot right out of the steamer and could be more than a little messy without the egg cup! It also does a great job hard boiling eggs too, also saving time and water. I've used this little gizmo 4 or 5 times a week for 2 1/2 years and it's still steaming away. The tray that heats the water needs to be wiped down pretty often as the steam/water removes the "bloom" from the eggshell and, combined with the chemicals in the water, can get a little crusty. However, that crusty stuff stays on the bottom and doesn't touch the egg inside the shell and comes off easily with a little soapy soak. I don't use a micro-wave and won't cook food in plastic, but since these eggs stay in the shell and nothing plastic touches the inner-egg it's about as pristine a process as I can make it, conserves energy, cuts the wait time considerably and makes perfect soft-boiled and hard-boiled eggs! It's a genius little cooker! (I gave 7 to friends and family for Christmas! ) So simple and inexpensive! Read more Read more

title: Powerful Upgrade: My Experience with the Ninja Blender
body: After years of using my Magic Bullet, I decided it was time for an upgrade and chose a Ninja blender because Ive always known Ninja to be a higher-quality brand. One of the main reasons I switched was that I wanted a larger cup size so I could make bigger smoothies without having to split them into multiple batches. The price of this blender also felt very fair for the quality and brand reputation, which made the decision even easier. One thing I really appreciate about this blender is how straightforward it is to use. There is essentially no assembly required and no complicated buttons to figure out. You simply screw the blade topper onto the cup, place it onto the machine, and press down to start blending. The only downside to this design is that you cant operate it hands-freeyou do have to hold it in place while it blends. That said, it works incredibly fast. It didnt even take a full minute to completely blend frozen fruit, ice, and the other ingredients in my smoothie. Another feature I liked is that this set came with three cups and multiple lids, including tops that make it easy to drink your smoothie on the go. Having the extra cups is convenient if youre making drinks for more than one person or prepping ahead. Overall, Im really happy with this purchase and feel like it was a worthwhile upgrade from my Magic Bullet. Read more Read more

product_name: Hamilton Beach Power Elite Wave Action Blender For Smoothies, 40 Oz Glass Jar, 12 Functions Including Puree, Crush Ice, 700 Watts - Black (58148A)
title: Great blender, so far - and a recipe for a delicious smoothie!
body: I picked this up after being unable to find a blender under $30 in any local stores that had decent reviews (I always check Amazon.com for pricing and reviews before purchasing anything). I had purchased a VERY similar model (same brand, and similar features) on impulse at a local store for right at $30, but decided not to even take it out of the box after reading the reviews on Amazon (my phone was dead when I purchased it, or I would have checked the reviews before pulling the trigger). After a bit of research I came across this unit which was a few dollars less, and is the same brand with almost identical features - so I was surprised that it had so much better reviews, but I figured it was worth the gamble and I needed a new blender for my morning smoothies ASAP (my old one was not working at-all). I never took the other one out of the box, so I can't compare this one to that one - but I can say that I'm very happy with this blender - it works perfectly for what I need it for, and although I have only had it for a few weeks, I haven't had any problems yet and it seems sturdy enough to last for a while. The problem with my last blender was that the gears the connected the motor in the base to the blades in the pitcher were made of rubber or soft plastic, so over the years they eventually got stripped until they were unable to catch each other enough to make the blades spin. During my search, I came across several models that had metal pieces connecting the blades to the motor which seems like it would be ideal. This model uses plastic, however it seems thick and sturdy enough to last a while. The pitcher is also very wide all the way to the bottom. Many blenders have a 'funnel' shaped pitcher that is smaller at the bottom (by the blades), sometimes causing ice or frozen chunks to get caught in the blades which can cause problems with the motor and/or the gears that connect the blades to the motor. Because the bottom of this is so wide, it seems like this happens less frequently because the blades will simply push the frozen chunks aside rather than getting them stuck between the blades and the glass. I rarely used the lid on my old blender, as I don't usually have issues with the ingredients splashing out - however, the lid of this blender has a couple of features that I really like. One is the spout that allows you to more easily pour your smoothies into your cups with less mess/dripping, and the other is a little 'flap' that allows you to easily pour in ingredients while blending without worrying about splashing. product_name: Hamilton Beach Power Elite Wave Action Blender For Smoothies, 40 Oz Glass Jar, 12 Functions Including Puree, Crush Ice, 700 Watts - Black (58148A)
title: Wish I had returned this
body: Updating from Oct 2024 (purchased July 2023): This blender really isnt good, or at least maybe I got a dud. Currently my blender only works 1/5 times and I have to finagle it around to get it to start. A weird smoky burning smell happens when its running as well. It also leaves odd black marks all over my (white!) countertops, and I cant figure out if this is the rubber on the bottom or if literally something is coming out of it. Its basically just a paper weight at this point. And all I use it for is making smoothies. It rarely even blends up all the fruit. Not a great use of my money even if it was a good price. Its a loss if you have to replace it every year. Ugh. Original: I don't know about this blender. I bought it during Prime Day because my old blender (a Black & Decker one I got in college and probably got on a Black Friday deal) had recently broken. There are so many blender options available that it is quite overwhelming to pick one especially with all the features they have, but since my old B&D was "basic," I figured I didn't need like a Ninja-level blender for my purposes (I literally plan to only make things like smoothies or milkshakes with this). The only requirement I wanted was a blender with a glass jar. this is just what I am used to , but I also noticed that many blenders with plastic jars (including a ninja one I was considering) had reviews that stated plastic jars may crack. So, with that being part of my search criteria, I saw this Hamilton Beach one for an ok price and it had many positive reviews, so I decided to pick it up. With that said, when I received this blender I already missed my B&D one in "look and feel." While I think the glass jar is a nice size/shape, I don't like the quality of the lid. I thought having a pour spout would be nice, but it just feels so flimsy and cheap and when you use it it's like another messy thing to clean. I'm not sure what it adds on just pouring straight from the glass pitcher. Plus I find it cheap and plastic-y and it is difficult to pull off of the glass jar (I've literally damaged my nails trying to release it). In terms of function, I don't think this is all that powerful. I have probably used it about 5-7 times so far, and I'd say 40% of the time I've had to "assist" the blender to process my food because it will get stuck. I have literally only made smoothies with this (and that is all I really intend to use it for). I followed the directions, saying put in liquids first, followed by fruit. My blender literally only has milk, yogurt, and frozen fruit, and it is not able to effectively blend that consistently without me having to open it and push the fruit down with a spoon. The "wave" action doesn't seem to do much. It is also very loud. It think it is louder when it is probably working harder (like when it is blending and not pulling the fruit down). But it really sounds like a vacuum cleaner. So that is annoying because I didn't have to stop and assist the blender on my old B&D. product_name: Hamilton Beach Power Elite Wave Action Blender For Smoothies, 40 Oz Glass Jar, 12 Functions Including Puree, Crush Ice, 700 Watts - Black (58148A)
title: Good warranty service (at least in Canada!), good blender for the price point
body: I've used this blender more or less on a daily basis with frozen fruits and vegetables for about two months. I knew that I would be getting a lot of use out of it, and have had experience with both low end blenders (always disappointing) and high end blenders (from working at a smoothie making business), and was a little bit hesitant to purchase one at this price point, rather than investing in a higher end, more powerful one. However, I was happily surprised by both the quality and easy of use of this blender. It has a very solid glass jar (I've had bad experiences with cracks in the jars of cheaper plastic blenders). It also comes apart for easy cleaning and can go in the dishwasher. It's so easy to clean and used so frequently in my household that I rarely need to put it in the dishwasher unless I'm in a rush. The motor may not be as powerful as some more high end blenders, but as long as you make sure to start with a liquid base, it has no trouble blending frozen foods. The only caveat that I have is that, about a month into use, my unit started leaking. At first, I thought it was because I was putting it back together incorrectly, but then I noticed a small crack in the plastic/rubber ring in the cutter assembly. After wondering how difficult it would be to get the part replaced, I continued using it for a few weeks, but finally made the call to the Hamilton Beach office in Canada today. Read more Read more

product_name: Air Fryer Paper Liners, 120Pcs Air Fryer Disposable Liners, Non-Stick and Oil Proof for Easy Cleanup, 8” Square for 5-8 qt Basket by Baker's Signature
title: Easy clean-up, no leaking, works well, good price
body: Using these with my Cosori 5.8qt air fryer; the liner fits well. When inserting the liner into the preheated air fryer basket, be careful not to burn your hand. I use a spoon or other object to help place the liner flat in the preheated basket. I have used these several times over the past week and never had oil or grease leak through. Even when cooking hamburger meat, which is very greasy! Feels great to just take out the dirty liner and throw it away, leaving a clean air fryer basket for the next meal. Easy! Follow the directions and the liner should work well. Do not place the empty liner in the air fryer while preheating. Preheat the air fryer with nothing in the basket. When its ready to cook, put the liner in the basket and be sure to weigh down the liner with food. If there is not enough food on the liner the liner could fly into the heating element and catch on fire. So be sure to weigh down the liner with food first. Then you could start air frying. So far I have not had any burns/fires with these liners. Very pleased with this product. Because I dont need to wash the air fryer basket after meals, I am using the air fryer more often! Great product.

body: After I learned to season it quick like 7 times, and started using a bush and cup with a dap of oil on the side I kept around to wipe with oil when done, and learned to due to size I needed to have rag on edge of sink when dumping out water, and then I often but not always heat up water in electric kettle to pour in when done cooking in order to not shock it. I find after all this that I actually do basically no scrubbing, just a bit of scraping with the metal spatula while the hot water is in it. And then I use a silicone handle on one side I had gotten and I want to get the other side for the silicone side to hold but I just use a pot holder for the other side, thicker one. But I find now that this is an ideal pan to cook everything and anything and many things. I just leave it on top of my stove for daily use. And the weight, for the same reason a mechanics arms are big while not lifting, their muscles are big purely due to making the same motion every day repetitively with the wrench. Its called a hermetic stressor, the same way weight session (more stressful obviously) and a hot sauna, cold plunge, run, etc, are all good forms of stress that cause adaptation, So to does this just cause you to lift a heavier pot a few times a day as you cook. For vast majority of us its nothing. But I told my mom for instance that she should use it and she complained how heavy it was, and my brutal honest response was thats exactly why you need to be using it because its not like you are working out. It wont make you buff, just a bit heavier than a similiar size pan, but for the older crowd who find it important to get their exercises in at the pool and such, this is no different. Plus, once I learned to clean efficiently and season a couple times, its a god send of a pan. I love it. I just want to put that its too heavy criticisism that comes with cast iron in a new light. Your body adapts, allow it too gradually. I will at some point be adding a top to this, I just havent decided if I need to get the lodge glass one which would be nice or a silicone one for this, or just a cast iron one to keep the theme and look and durability forever. (Glass and silicone could both break in different ways). I do love this pan as I will admit, one of the reason I got this pan, being some one who can be hard on things by temperment, after knowing roughly how to care for this pan, I also have a lisence to absolutely abuse it and cant scratch it or anything. Because once you have researched a couple of ways to take rust off and to totally reseason and recondition an old used on for instance, you have the confidence to own this the rest of your life and not ever feel like your going to rune it. Im also strategically lazy, I call efficient, my mom thinks different, lol, but anyway, the fastest way to clean any pan is with hot water right when done cooking as it burns and melts stuff right off, I can do that with this without worrying how it affects coatings or anything and I can do that to kingdom come. I just try to throw water from kettle on it ideally but not always. Again, the point of these pans is the amazing non stick coating that develops after use and learning to season a few more times your self, but that you can absolutely abuse these pans and know they will last. product_name: Lodge Seasoned Cast Iron Skillet - PFAS-Free, Non-Toxic Cookware - Use with an Oven, Stove, Grill, or Campfire - Naturally Non-Stick & Oven Safe - 10.25 Inches
title: Timeless classic for the modern kitchen
body: Sorry for the long review - for the short review, count the stars! I'm a bit of a purist. I always season my cast iron - new, or used (hey, I don't know WHAT someone else used that old piece of cast iron for - maybe cleaning auto parts). I sand it down to bare metal, starting with about an 80 grit and finishing with 200. Then I season. The end result is a glossy black mirror that puts Teflon to shame. There are two mistakes people make when seasoning - not hot enough, not long enough. These mistakes give the same result - a sticky brown coating that is definitely not non-stick, and the first time they bring any real heat to the pan, clouds of smoke that they neither expected or wanted. I see several complaints here that are completely due to not knowing this. But there were a few pieces I needed (yes, needed, cast iron isn't about want, it's a need), and this was one of them, so I thought I'd give the Lodge pre-seasoning a try. Ordered last Friday, received this Friday - free shipping, yay! The first thing I noticed was the bumpy coating. The inside is actually rougher than the outside, and my hand was itching for the sandpaper, but that would have defeated the experiment. This time, I was going to give the Lodge pre-seasoning a chance before I broke out the sandpaper. So I scrubbed the pan out with a plastic brush and a little soapy water, rinsed well, put it on a medium burner, and waited. Cast iron tip number one - give it a little time. Then give it a little more time. Cast iron conducts heat much more slowly than aluminum, so you have to have a little patience. Read more Read more

title: I cook almost everything in my Lodge Cast Iron Pan
body: If you haven't already taken the leap into cast iron you should think about it. First off the pan is cheap, durable and generally non-stick. Does this mean nothing will stick to this pan? No but if you have the right temperature and use the right amount of fat/oil when cooking and take care of this pan it will take care of you. I would say this pan excels at proteins first and foremost. I have cooked bacon, eggs and pancakes right after one another and nothing stuck to this pan. I would say vegetables are also a favorite in this pan as well because of the great flavor this pan helps create through the oil and char you can achieve with this pan. If you are the type of person that cooks and then puts the pans in the sink for a later time then cast iron might not be for you but I have left this pan in a sink for a few hours or on the stove top overnight and cleaned the next day so it is worth a shot. If you are the type of person who lets pans soak in water overnight or puts them immediately into a dishwasher for cleaning then this pan is definitely not for you unless you are willing to make an exception with this pan. Did I have immediate success with this pan right away? No I didn't. I had food stick and I had issues with cleaning the pan and reseasoning the pan and had to look for advice on the internet to solve my issues because not many people use cast iron so I couldn't ask them. title: Love Lodge Cast Iron Skillets!!!
body: I bought 2 of the Lodge Seasoned Cast Iron Skillets 6.5 inches...I already had the 8 inch for a few years now and love it that i wanted some smaller ones for my toaster oven and making a skillet cookie since never in my life have made one...Last night i made a brown butter chocolate chip skillet cooking in this cast iron in my toaster oven and it was perfect...it cooked evenly. fit right into my Hamilton Beach 4 Slice counter top Toaster Oven with no problems..heating up nicely with no problems that i noticed...I sprayed it first with Baker's Joy baking spray with flour on the cast iron then pressed my homemade cookie dough into the cast iron pan without any problems in the toaster oven it went at 350 for 20 min and was nicely done... was easy to pull in and out of the toaster oven its not light as a feather but also not a ton bricks to pick up either has a nice weight to it...I see these lasting me for years to come...once all done I washed it in hot water little bit of dawn dish soap dried it really well right after washing and re seasoned it with carapelli unfiltered extra virgin olive oil... best olive oil and ive tried all kinds over the years and nope nothing compares to this extra virgin olive oil i def recommend trying it out... anyway was very easy to season again and the pre seasoning of it was fine i didn't notice any distinct smell or ruining of my baked good in it at all...it is sturdy and made of excellent quality def would recommend this cast iron pan ... can't wait to get and try out their bread loaf pans and def the cupcake / muffin pan....

title: Turn the unit off after your food is "Ready" or use "warm" setting
body: This air fryer is everything they say it is and I highly recommend it The ceramic non stick coating really sealed the deal for me However, I notice that when food is finished cooking or displays"Ready" if I don't turn the unit off after about 5 minutes I smell plastic The unit was set up and prepared for use as per instructions and I've already cooked with it a few times with no problems but I would always pull the basket out and push it back in, or turn the unit off after cooking I suspect the AC coil may still be activated without the fan running Without the DC fan running it could potentially heat up some plastic and if that AC coil does indeed remain active then I would suggest to turn the unit off or unplug the unit I will update if this happens again but I do know that I smelled plastic This could be a design flaw but only time will tell I would suggest to turn the unit off or unplug it when your food is "Ready" or use the "Warm" setting if you must let it sit This could be a big deal if this is indeed a design flaw Other than that, this is probably the best air fryer for the money Just remember to pull the drawer out and push it back in to in to reset the unit to idle Don't let it just sit there with the "Ready" light remaining on Just takes a second or less to tap on the screen to shut it off or pull the tray out and push it back in You could also use the "Warm" feature which does use the DC powered fan with the low heat I hope I am wrong about this because I really love this air fryer and the ceramic non stick coating that it has, and It cooks very very well Again, there really is no need to have the "Ready" light on with the coil still activated It should have an after cooking timer like the instant pot that tells you how long it's been sitting there for reference But the instant pot also has the keep warm feature turned on by default, so you would also have to turn that off or some amount of heat would still be used, but it is designed that way and explained in the users manual This Cosori unit is supposed to shut itself off after 30 minutes of inactivity, but not trusting in that if I smell any plastic fumes I am still keeping the unit despite this potential design flaw I can live with that when everything else about this unit is perfectly fine Read more Read more

title: Outstanding Device, Cosori!
body: Because we ran somewhat late after working in the yard, we decided to cook frozen Fish Sticks and French Fries in this Air Fryer for our first use of it. Our first reaction was, WOW! This air fryer took these simple foods to another level! Our fish sticks & fries turned out crisp and flavorful; better than we've ever had them before. In fact, we didn't even realize these quick, easy foods could be that good. Prior to getting this Air Fryer, we had always prepared our fish & fries in our big oven at 420 degrees. And, they always took over 20 minutes each; so the fries would be cooled and somewhat wrinkled and shrunken, while trying to keep them warm in the small microwave warmer as we cooked the fish sticks. With this Air Fryer, the fries were done in about 15 minutes and the fish sticks about 12 min; a little over 25 mines combined. We were astonished at how much oil drained out of the sticks & fries through the crisper plate to the bottom of the fryer basket. We didn't realize that these frozen foods had so much oil in them, and how much they retained, when lying in their own oil on a flat cookie sheet to heat. We just wish you had the same model and features with a 7 or 8 qt. size basket that would easily allow cooking a whole 6-7lb chicken or turkey. Lastly, we very much appreciated how effortless the non-stick ceramic crisper plate fryer drawer cleaned up... simple! Outstanding food prep device Cosori! Something to be proud of!

title: Amazing, but gigantic. Gave to my partner.
body: 5 stars because I got this bottle on sale for the same price as the 30oz, the colors are *gorgeous* and every bit as vibrant as the image shows (which I'm seeing is not typical across the board for owala) and it is indeed just a fabulous, strong water bottle. Easy to drink from the built-in straw, a joy to carry, and fits in every cup holder so far. I've already adapted better hydrating habits compared to using my old daily Nalgene. However, I am 5'5 and wanted a bottle to carry around frequently for work. This thing is like as big as my thigh bone lol! I should have gone to a local Target or Academy to compare the 30oz vs the 40oz bottle because the size difference is significant. If you consider the added weight for a full 40oz, I set myself up for failure in terms of my needs for this bottle. My 6'2" boyfriend who loves colors and works outside all day was happy to adopt this one for me however, and I ended up purchasing a 30oz elsewhere in equally fun colors. We have both had success having ice last a full workday. His keeps cold in the car and mine usually has a good bit of ice still after 3 refills and constant use. So far so good on no-spills, and I really appreciate the lock mechanism as I work around delicate artwork all day. It would be nice if the cups were machine washable alongside the gaskets, lid, and straw-- but alas, it's easy enough to clean in the sink and probably wouldn't fit in a top rack anyway. Each bottle comes wrapped with beautifully thorough care and use instructions as well-- take the time to read it all! Read more Read more

The rugged flip handle is also a convenient way to carry the Owala when it's not in the water bottle holder on my backpack. I hand wash the body of the bottle, but the top disassembles easily and can go right in the dishwasher when I want a deep cleaning. The bottle has a wide opening, so it is very easy to clean the inside. This has quickly become my favorite water bottle due to its ease of cleaning, good insulation, ability to fit in my cup holder, solid construction and materials, and the option of using the straw or chug spout. I have the 30 ounce bottle, but I am going to be picking up a 40 ouncer also. You won't regret purchasing one! title: Amazing Pot, my favorite appliance ever.
body: I received my Instant Pot right before the end of the year as I had pre-ordered it. I had done a good amount of research on the previous model and was convinced it could replace my small slow cooker as well as diversify my cooking and reduce my time in the kitchen. The main improvements I liked on this model over the previous model was the holder for the lid on both sides of the handles of the pot, the larger display, and the main improvement I was interested in was the dual pressure setting (mainly comes in handy for veggies for me). At first use it took a little getting used to this new appliance. Some of the features which take some getting used to for me were the pressure release valve, and how to use the electronic controls. When cooking in a pressure cooker using traditional methods it is necessary to set the pressure release valve to closed, this allows pressure to build up in the pot during cooking. During the first few times I had forgotten to twist the pressure release valve to closed. Not doing this will eventually cause a light whistling sound and steam to come out of the release. Twist it back and all is well. I mention this because I read a review giving this pot a 1 star rating, and I could tell they hadn't closed their pressure valve based on the results they said they achieved. Read more Read more

product_name: Instant Pot Duo 7-in-1 Electric Pressure Cooker, Slow Cooker, Rice, Steamer, Sauté, Yogurt Maker, Warmer & Sterilizer, Includes App With Over 800 Recipes, Stainless Steel, 6 Quart
title: I LOVE My Instant Pot! But Here's What I Wish I'd Known when I First Got It
body: I LOVE my Instant Pot! But I will be the first to admit that it can be a little intimidating at first, and it can feel like it has a steep learning curve (and I'm a tech reviewer and tech lawyer, and one of those people who generally just jumps in and figures things out without reading the manuals ("Manuals? We don't need no stinkin' manuals!"), so for me to feel like I'm not 'getting' something ..well, yeah. So if you are feeling a little bewildered by your new Instant Pot, *you're not alone*!) Plus, the manual does *not* include certain information that I, at least, was looking for. Such as, how long is each pre-programmed cooking cycle? Exactly what temperature do the various saut settings heat to? Things like that. So, here are a few tips that have really helped me to finally 'get' it, plus instructions for two things that you can make in your Instant Pot that will change your life: incredibly easy perfectly poached eggs in 2-3 minutes, and baked potatoes in 12 minutes. First, it is almost impossible to mess up with this thing to a point of being dangerous, so if you're concerned about the exploding pressure cookers of yore, you needn't be (I said "almost", don't go overriding your pot's safety features and then blame me when you poke an eye out). The lid audibly tells you when its sealed (when you turn it clockwise), and the pot won't even build up much pressure if you haven't properly closed the steam release handle by turning it, too, clockwise. The most likely point at which a problem could arise would be if you try to open the lid (by turning it counter-clockwise) before all of the pressure has been released and normalized (so don't do that). The pot visually lets you know when it's safe to open the pot, by the float valve (the little silver post that pops up when the pot is pressurized) dropping back down flush with the lid instead of being popped up. Think of the float valve as the reverse of a turkey pop-up button, in the case of the float valve it's done when the button pops *in*, instead of out. Read more Read more

title: A truly Canadian innovation - Instant Pot is the best item in my kitchen, hands down
body: Why I Bought The Instant Pot (and why you probably should, too)  In this review, I'll cover safety mechanisms and info, how to use the instant pot, minimum liquid requirements, along with a whole slew of things I learned over a year and a half of ownership. I waited a long time to write this because I wanted to be sure the Instant Pot wasn't a flash in the pan gimmick. How was the quality of construction? Did it maintain heating standards over the years? How often do parts need replacement? I had so many questions! The technology was too new for definitive answers. Yes, it has thousands of positive reviews, but so many people tend to review the first moment they get a product and never update. I wanted answer, so I took the plunge FOR SCIENCE! (Also delicious food, but mostly science) I love food, and I love cooking. While not amazing, I am a passionate home cook with a wide variety of culinary interests. I make meals for my wife daily, and I refuse to buy cheap, nutritionally absent lunches. As such, I'm almost always in the kitchen plugging away. The truth is... the Instant Pot is brilliant, and the high praise it has received from reviewers is SPOT ON. It has made everything in my culinary life easier and better. I've used it constantly over a year and a half (originally purchased August, 2016), and it looks like it's fresh off the assembly line. I haven't even had to replace the least durable aspect (the silicon seal) due to my lack of acidic recipes.
